revision.text:
{{Infobox Ägyptische Gottheit |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[Altes Reich (Ägypten)|Altes Reich]] |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''{{unicode|ḥb-ẖnp}}-š[[Datei:U-A725 LATIN SMALL LETTER EGYPTOLOGICAL AIN.gif|7px]]''
''Fest des Sandschiebens / Sandholens''
''Fest des Sandausschüttens'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = '''Festdatum''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION =''{{Unicode|3ḫt}}'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Zweiter Tag des Mondes im zweiten Monat der Periode Achet'' |BILD1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Sesostris-II.-Pyramide in Al-Lahun }} Das Fest '''Chenep-scha''' (auch '''Tag des Sandholens''') ist seit dem [[Altes Reich (Ägypten)|Alten Reich]] unter [[Niuserre]] belegt. Es steht in Verbindung mit der Geburt des [[Horus]] und der im [[Mittleres Reich|Mittleren Reich]] in [[Theben (Ägypten)|Theben]] bezeugten Göttin [[Wosret]], die als Schützerin des jugendlichen Horus verehrt wurde. == Hintergrund == === Altes Reich === Das Fest Chenep-scha lag nicht auf dem sonst üblichen ersten Tag des [[Neumond]]es, sondern beinhaltete das [[Ritus|Ritual]] vom ''Füllen des [[Horusauge]]s'' am zweiten Mondmonatstag. Das [[Menchet-Fest]] folgte einen Tag später am dritten Tag und symbolisierte das ''Bekleiden des [[Mond]]es''. === 12. Dynastie === In der [[12. Dynastie]] wurde unter [[Sesostris III.]] auch die Bezeichnung ''Tag des Sandholens des Anubis auf seinem Berge in der Pyramidenstadt von Sesostris II., des Seligen'' geführt. Die [[Sesostris-II.-Pyramide]] von [[Al-Lahun]] galt mit ihrem Standort als traditionelle Zuweisung zum ''[[Anubis (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Berg des Anubis]]''. Aus dem Tempeltagebuch ([[Papyrus Berlin 10248]]) im 14. Regierungsjahr des Sesostris III. ist die Verkündung im [[Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|Mondkalender]] für den [[Achet|18. Achet II]] am zweiten Mondtag vermerkt. Da im [[Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe]] aus dem 35. Regierungsjahr des [[Amenemhet III.]] das Menchet-Fest für den zweiten Monat Achet ohne Datumsnennung angesetzt war, lag eine Verkündung des Mondtermins zum Zeitpunkt der Erstellung des damaligen Festkalenders noch nicht vor. == Literatur == * Rainer Hannig: ''Großes Handwörterbuch Ägyptisch-Deutsch : (2800 - 950 v. Chr.)''. von Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. S. 649. * Rolf Krauss: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'', Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}} * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/Wiesbaden 1950 {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Kategorie:Feste und Brauchtum (Altägypten)]]

{{Infobox Ägyptische Gottheit |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[Altes Reich (Ägypten)|Altes Reich]] |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''{{unicode|ḥb-ẖnp}}-š[[Datei:U-A725 LATIN SMALL LETTER EGYPTOLOGICAL AIN.gif|7px]]''
''Fest des Sandschiebens / Sandholens''
''Fest des Sandausschüttens'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = '''Festdatum''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION =''{{Unicode|3ḫt}}'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Zweiter Tag des Mondes im zweiten Monat der Periode Achet'' |BILD1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Sesostris-II.-Pyramide in Al-Lahun }} Das Fest '''Chenep-scha''' (auch '''Tag des Sandholens''') ist seit dem [[Altes Reich (Ägypten)|Alten Reich]] unter [[Niuserre]] belegt. Es steht in Verbindung mit der Geburt des [[Horus]] und der im [[Mittleres Reich|Mittleren Reich]] in [[Theben (Ägypten)|Theben]] bezeugten Göttin [[Wosret]], die als Schützerin des jugendlichen Horus verehrt wurde. == Hintergrund == === Altes Reich === Das Fest Chenep-scha lag nicht auf dem sonst üblichen ersten Tag des [[Neumond]]es, sondern beinhaltete das [[Ritus|Ritual]] vom ''Füllen des [[Horusauge]]s'' am zweiten Mondmonatstag. Das [[Menchet-Fest]] folgte einen Tag später am dritten Tag und symbolisierte das ''Bekleiden des [[Mond]]es''. === 12. Dynastie === In der [[12. Dynastie]] wurde unter [[Sesostris III.]] auch die Bezeichnung ''Tag des Sandholens des Anubis auf seinem Berge in der Pyramidenstadt von Sesostris II., des Seligen'' geführt. Die [[Sesostris-II.-Pyramide]] von [[Al-Lahun]] galt mit ihrem Standort als traditionelle Zuweisung zum ''[[Anubis (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Berg des Anubis]]''. Aus dem Tempeltagebuch ([[Papyrus Berlin 10248]]) im 14. Regierungsjahr des Sesostris III. ist die Verkündung im [[Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|Mondkalender]] für den [[Achet|18. Achet II]] am zweiten Mondtag vermerkt. Da im [[Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe]] aus dem 35. Regierungsjahr des [[Amenemhet III.]] das Menchet-Fest für den zweiten Monat Achet ohne Datumsnennung angesetzt war, lag eine Verkündung des Mondtermins zum Zeitpunkt der Erstellung des damaligen Festkalenders noch nicht vor. == Literatur == * Rainer Hannig: ''Großes Handwörterbuch Ägyptisch-Deutsch : (2800 - 950 v. Chr.)''. von Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. S. 649. * Rolf Krauss: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'', Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}} * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/Wiesbaden 1950 {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Kategorie:Feste und Brauchtum (Altägypten)]] [[Kategorie:Festkalender des Monats Achet II]]

Da im [[Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe]] aus dem 35. Regierungsjahr des [[Amenemhet III.]] das Menchet-Fest für den zweiten Monat Achet ohne Datumsnennung angesetzt war, lag eine Verkündung des Mondtermins zum Zeitpunkt der Erstellung des damaligen Festkalenders noch nicht vor. == Literatur == * Rainer Hannig: ''Großes Handwörterbuch Ägyptisch-Deutsch : (2800 - 950 v. S. 649. * Rolf Krauss: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'', Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}} * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten''. Es steht in Verbindung mit der Geburt des [[Horus]] und der im [[Mittleres Reich|Mittleren Reich]] in [[Theben (Ägypten)|Theben]] bezeugten Göttin [[Wosret]], die als Schützerin des jugendlichen Horus verehrt wurde. == Hintergrund == === Altes Reich === Das Fest Chenep-scha lag nicht auf dem sonst üblichen ersten Tag des [[Neumond]]es, sondern beinhaltete das [[Ritus|Ritual]] vom ''Füllen des [[Horusauge]]s'' am zweiten Mondmonatstag. Das [[Menchet-Fest]] folgte einen Tag später am dritten Tag und symbolisierte das ''Bekleiden des [[Mond]]es''. === 12. Dynastie === In der [[12. Dynastie]] wurde unter [[Sesostris III.]] auch die Bezeichnung ''Tag des Sandholens des Anubis auf seinem Berge in der Pyramidenstadt von Sesostris II., des Seligen'' geführt. Die [[Sesostris-II.-Pyramide]] von [[Al-Lahun]] galt mit ihrem Standort als traditionelle Zuweisung zum ''[[Anubis (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Berg des Anubis]]''. Aus dem Tagebuch des [[Totentempel des Sesostris II.|Totentempels von Sesostris II.]] ([[Papyrus Berlin 10248]]) ist im 14. Regierungsjahr des Sesostris III. die Verkündung im [[Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|Mondkalender]] für den [[Achet|18. Achet II]] am zweiten Mondtag vermerkt. Da im [[Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe]] aus dem 35. Regierungsjahr des [[Amenemhet III.]] das Menchet-Fest für den zweiten Monat Achet ohne Datumsnennung angesetzt war, lag eine Verkündung des Mondtermins zum Zeitpunkt der Erstellung des damaligen Festkalenders noch nicht vor. == Literatur == * [[Rainer Hannig]]: ''Großes Handwörterbuch Ägyptisch – Deutsch. (2800 – 950 v. Chr.). Die Sprache der Pharaonen'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Bd. 1 = ''[[Kulturgeschichte der antiken Welt]].'' Bd. 64). Marburger Edition, 4. überarbeitete Auflage. von Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. S. 649. * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer ägyptologische Beiträge. (HÄB).'' Bd. 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse'' 1950, 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur u. a., Mainz u. a. 1950. {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Kategorie:Feste und Brauchtum (Altägypten)]] [[Kategorie:Festkalender des Monats Achet II]]

Da im [[Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe]] aus dem 35. Regierungsjahr des [[Amenemhet III.]] das Menchet-Fest für den zweiten Monat Achet ohne Datumsnennung angesetzt war, lag eine Verkündung des Mondtermins zum Zeitpunkt der Erstellung des damaligen Festkalenders noch nicht vor. == Literatur == * [[Rainer Hannig]]: ''Großes Handwörterbuch Ägyptisch – Deutsch. (2800 – 950 v. Chr.). Die Sprache der Pharaonen'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Bd. 1 = ''[[Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt]].'' Bd. 64). Marburger Edition, 4. überarbeitete Auflage. von Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. S. 649. * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer ägyptologische Beiträge. (HÄB).'' Bd. 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Es steht in Verbindung mit der Geburt des [[Horus]] und der im [[Mittleres Reich|Mittleren Reich]] in [[Theben (Ägypten)|Theben]] bezeugten Göttin [[Wosret]], die als Schützerin des jugendlichen Horus verehrt wurde. == Hintergrund == === Altes Reich === Das Fest Chenep-scha lag nicht auf dem sonst üblichen ersten Tag des [[Neumond]]es, sondern beinhaltete das [[Ritus|Ritual]] vom ''Füllen des [[Horusauge]]s'' am zweiten Mondmonatstag. Das [[Menchet-Fest]] folgte einen Tag später am dritten Tag und symbolisierte das ''Bekleiden des [[Mond]]es''. === 12. Dynastie === In der [[12. Dynastie]] wurde unter [[Sesostris III.]] auch die Bezeichnung ''Tag des Sandholens des Anubis auf seinem Berge in der Pyramidenstadt von Sesostris II., des Seligen'' geführt. Die [[Sesostris-II.-Pyramide]] von [[Al-Lahun]] galt mit ihrem Standort als traditionelle Zuweisung zum ''Berg des [[Anubis]]''. ''Fest des Sandschiebens / Sandholens''
''Fest des Sandausschüttens'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = '''Festdatum''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION =''3ḫt'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Zweiter Tag des Mondes im zweiten Monat der Periode Achet'' |BILD1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Sesostris-II.-Pyramide in Al-Lahun }} Das Fest '''Chenep-scha''' (auch '''Tag des Sandholens''') ist seit dem [[Altes Reich (Ägypten)|Alten Reich]] unter König ([[Pharao]]) [[Niuserre]] belegt. Es steht in Verbindung mit der Geburt des [[Horus]] und der im [[Mittleres Reich|Mittleren Reich]] in [[Theben (Ägypten)|Theben]] bezeugten Göttin [[Wosret]], die als Schützerin des jugendlichen Horus verehrt wurde. == Hintergrund == === Altes Reich === Das Fest Chenep-scha lag nicht auf dem sonst üblichen ersten Tag des [[Neumond]]es, sondern beinhaltete das [[Ritus|Ritual]] vom ''Füllen des [[Horusauge]]s'' am zweiten Mondmonatstag. Das [[Menchet-Fest]] folgte einen Tag später am dritten Tag und symbolisierte das ''Bekleiden des [[Mond]]es''. === 12. Dynastie === In der [[12. Dynastie]] wurde unter [[Sesostris III.]] auch die Bezeichnung ''Tag des Sandholens des Anubis auf seinem Berge in der Pyramidenstadt von Sesostris II., des Seligen'' geführt. Die [[Sesostris-II.-Pyramide]] von [[Al-Lahun]] galt mit ihrem Standort als traditionelle Zuweisung zum ''Berg des [[Anubis]]''. Aus dem Tagebuch des [[Totentempel des Sesostris II.|Totentempels von Sesostris II.]] ([[Papyrus Berlin 10248]]) ist im 14. Regierungsjahr des Sesostris III. die Verkündung im [[Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|Mondkalender]] für den [[Achet|18. Achet II]] am zweiten Mondtag vermerkt. Da im [[Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe]] aus dem 35. Regierungsjahr des [[Amenemhet III.]] das Menchet-Fest für den zweiten Monat Achet ohne Datumsnennung angesetzt war, lag eine Verkündung des Mondtermins zum Zeitpunkt der Erstellung des damaligen Festkalenders noch nicht vor. == Literatur == * [[Rainer Hannig]]: ''Großes Handwörterbuch Ägyptisch – Deutsch. (2800 – 950 v. Chr.). Die Sprache der Pharaonen'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Band 1 = ''[[Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt]].'' Band 64). Marburger Edition, 4. überarbeitete Auflage, von Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. S. 649. * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer ägyptologische Beiträge. (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur u. a., Mainz u. a. 1950. {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Kategorie:Religiöses Fest (Altes Ägypten)]] [[Kategorie:Festkalender des Monats Achet II]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[de:Chenep-scha]]

{{Improve||This article|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Discussion: Menchet-Fest|ja|Diskussion:Menchet-Fest|lt=Menchet|lt_ja=Menchet}}) The article does not yet contain any individual citation.}} {{Infobox Hieroglyphs Two-Column |EXTENDED NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|lt=Old Kingdom|lt_ja=Altes Reich}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Sand Shifting / Sand Lifting''
''Festival of Sand Dispersion'' |EXTENDED NAME2 = '''Festival Date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION = ''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second day of the moon in the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Sesostris II Pyramid in Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also known as '''Day of Sand Lifting''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|ja|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|lt=Old Kingdom|lt_ja=Alten Reich}} under the reign of King ({{ill|Pharaoh|ja|Pharao}}) {{ill|Niuserre|ja|Niuserre}}. It is associated with the birth of {{ill|Horus|ja|Horus}} and the goddess {{ill|Wosret|ja|Wosret}}, attested in the {{ill|Middle Kingdom|ja|Mittleres Reich|lt=Middle Kingdom|lt_ja=Mittleren Reich}} at {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|ja|Theben (Ägypten)|lt=Thebes|lt_ja=Theben}}, who was venerated as the protector of the youthful Horus. ==Background== ===Old Kingdom=== The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the otherwise customary first day of the {{ill|New Moon|ja|Neumond}}, but instead included the {{ill|Ritual|ja|Ritus|lt=Ritual|lt_ja=Ritual}} of the ''Filling of the {{ill|Eye of Horus|ja|Horusauge}}'' on the second day of the lunar month. The {{ill|Menchet Festival|ja|Menchet-Fest}} followed one day later on the third day and symbolized the ''Dressing of the {{ill|Moon|ja|Mond}}''. ===12th Dynasty=== In the {{ill|12th Dynasty|ja|12. Dynastie}}, under {{ill|Sesostris III|ja|Sesostris III.}}, the designation ''Day of the Sand Lifting of {{ill|Anubis|ja|Anubis}} on his mountain in the pyramid city of Sesostris II, the Blessed'' was also used. The {{ill|Sesostris II Pyramid|ja|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide}} of {{ill|Al-Lahun|ja|Al-Lahun}} was traditionally assigned, by virtue of its location, to the ''Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|ja|Anubis}}''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|ja|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|lt=Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|lt_ja=Totentempels von Sesostris II.}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|Papyrus Berlin 10248}}) it is recorded in the 14th regnal year of Sesostris III that the announcement in the {{ill|Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|ja|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|lt=Lunar Calendar|lt_ja=Mondkalender}} for {{ill|Achet|ja|Achet|lt=18th Achet II|lt_ja=18. Achet II}} on the second lunar day was made. Since in the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|ja|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhet III|ja|Amenemhet III.}} the Menchet Festival for the second month Achet was scheduled without a specified date, an announcement of the lunar date was not yet available at the time of the creation of that festival calendar. ==Literature== * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|ja|Rainer Hannig}}: ''Great Hand Dictionary of Egyptian – German. (2800 – 950 BC). The Language of the Pharaohs'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = {{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|ja|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt}}. Volume 64). Marburg Edition, 4th revised edition, by Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|ja|Rolf Krauss}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Dates. Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt'' (= ''Hildesheim Egyptological Contributions (HÄB)''. Volume 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Incorrect ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|Siegfried Schott}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Memoirs of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class (AM-GS)''. 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz et al. 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Festival Calendar of the Month Achet II]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Pages to be translated]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Chenep-scha]] [[Category:qq]] [[de:Chenep-scha]] [[Category:Completion Tier 1]][[ja:Chenep-scha]]

It is associated with the birth of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス}} and the goddess {{ill|Wosret|de|Wosret|ja|ウォスレット}}, attested in the {{ill|Middle Kingdom|de|Mittleres Reich|ja|エジプト中王国}} at {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|de|Theben (Ägypten)|ja|テーベ (エジプト)}}, who was venerated as the protector of the youthful Horus. ==Background== ===Old Kingdom=== The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the otherwise customary first day of the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月}}, but instead included the {{ill|Ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式}} of the ''Filling of the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Horusauge|ja|ホルスの目}}'' on the second day of the lunar month. Dynastie|ja|第12王朝}}, under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.|ja|セソストリスIII}}, the designation ''Day of the Sand Lifting of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス}} on his mountain in the pyramid city of Sesostris II, the Blessed'' was also used. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|セソストリスIIの葬祭寺}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリンパピルス10248}}) it is recorded in the 14th regnal year of Sesostris III that the announcement in the {{ill|Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|ja|古代エジプトの月暦}} for {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アチェット}} on the second lunar day was made. Since in the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|アクロバット団の祭日暦}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhet III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメネムヘトIII}} the Menchet Festival for the second month Achet was scheduled without a specified date, an announcement of the lunar date was not yet available at the time of the creation of that festival calendar. ==Literature== * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|ja|ライナー・ハンニグ}}: ''Great Hand Dictionary of Egyptian – German. (2800 – 950 BC). The Language of the Pharaohs'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = {{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史}}. Volume 64). Marburg Edition, 4th revised edition, by Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Dates. Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Incorrect ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Since in the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|アクロバット団の祭日暦}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhet III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメネムヘトIII}} the Menchet Festival for the second month Achet was scheduled without a specified date, an announcement of the lunar date was not yet available at the time of the creation of that festival calendar. ==Literature== * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|ja|ライナー・ハンニグ}}: ''Great Hand Dictionary of Egyptian – German. (2800 – 950 BC). The Language of the Pharaohs'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = {{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史}}. Volume 64). Marburg Edition, 4th revised edition, by Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Dates. Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt'' (= ''Hildesheim Egyptological Contributions (HÄB)''. Volume 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Incorrect ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Memoirs of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class (AM-GS)''. 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz et al. 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Festival Calendar of the Month Achet II]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Pages to be translated]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Chenep-scha]] [[Category:qq]] [[de:Chenep-scha]] [[Category:Completion Tier 1]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[sn:Chenep-scha]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[ja:Chenep-scha]]

revision.text:
{{Improve||This article|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Discussion: Menchet-Fest|de|Diskussion: Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭の議論}}) The article does not yet contain any individual citation.}} {{Infobox Hieroglyphs Two-Column |EXTENDED NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Sand Shifting / Sand Lifting''
''Festival of Sand Dispersion'' |EXTENDED NAME2 = '''Festival Date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION = ''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second day of the moon in the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Sesostris II Pyramid in Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also known as '''Day of Sand Lifting''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国}} under the reign of King ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ}}) {{ill|Niuserre|de|Niuserre|ja|ニウセルレ}}. It is associated with the birth of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス}} and the goddess {{ill|Wosret|de|Wosret|ja|ウォスレット}}, attested in the {{ill|Middle Kingdom|de|Mittleres Reich|ja|エジプト中王国}} at {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|de|Theben (Ägypten)|ja|テーベ (エジプト)}}, who was venerated as the protector of the youthful Horus. ==Background== ===Old Kingdom=== The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the otherwise customary first day of the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月}}, but instead included the {{ill|Ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式}} of the ''Filling of the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Horusauge|ja|ホルスの目}}'' on the second day of the lunar month. The {{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭}} followed one day later on the third day and symbolized the ''Dressing of the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月}}''. ===12th Dynasty=== In the {{ill|12th Dynasty|de|12. Dynastie|ja|第12王朝}}, under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.|ja|セソストリスIII}}, the designation ''Day of the Sand Lifting of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス}} on his mountain in the pyramid city of Sesostris II, the Blessed'' was also used. The {{ill|Sesostris II Pyramid|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|ja|セソストリスIIのピラミッド}} of {{ill|Al-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|ja|アル・ラフーン}} was traditionally assigned, by virtue of its location, to the ''Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス}}''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|セソストリスIIの葬祭寺}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリンパピルス10248}}) it is recorded in the 14th regnal year of Sesostris III that the announcement in the {{ill|Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|ja|古代エジプトの月暦}} for {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アチェット}} on the second lunar day was made. Since in the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|アクロバット団の祭日暦}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhet III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメネムヘトIII}} the Menchet Festival for the second month Achet was scheduled without a specified date, an announcement of the lunar date was not yet available at the time of the creation of that festival calendar. ==Literature== * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|ja|ライナー・ハンニグ}}: ''Great Hand Dictionary of Egyptian – German. (2800 – 950 BC). The Language of the Pharaohs'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = {{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史}}. Volume 64). Marburg Edition, 4th revised edition, by Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Dates. Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt'' (= ''Hildesheim Egyptological Contributions (HÄB)''. Volume 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Incorrect ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Memoirs of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class (AM-GS)''. 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz et al. 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Festival Calendar of the Month Achet II]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Pages to be translated]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Chenep-scha]] [[Category:qq]] [[de:Chenep-scha]] [[Category:Completion Tier 1]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[sn:Chenep-scha]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[ja:Chenep-scha]]

revision.text:
{{Improve||This article|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Discussion: Menchet-Fest|de|Diskussion: Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭の議論}}) The article does not yet contain any individual citation.}} {{Infobox Hieroglyphs Two-Column |EXTENDED NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Sand Shifting / Sand Lifting''
''Festival of Sand Dispersion'' |EXTENDED NAME2 = '''Festival Date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION = ''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second day of the moon in the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Sesostris II Pyramid in Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also known as '''Day of Sand Lifting''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国}} under the reign of King ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ}}) {{ill|Niuserre|de|Niuserre|ja|ニウセルレ}}. It is associated with the birth of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス}} and the goddess {{ill|Wosret|de|Wosret|ja|ウォスレット}}, attested in the {{ill|Middle Kingdom|de|Mittleres Reich|ja|エジプト中王国}} at {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|de|Theben (Ägypten)|ja|テーベ (エジプト)}}, who was venerated as the protector of the youthful Horus. ==Background== ===Old Kingdom=== The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the otherwise customary first day of the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月}}, but instead included the {{ill|Ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式}} of the ''Filling of the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Horusauge|ja|ホルスの目}}'' on the second day of the lunar month. The {{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭}} followed one day later on the third day and symbolized the ''Dressing of the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月}}''. ===12th Dynasty=== In the {{ill|12th Dynasty|de|12. Dynastie|ja|第12王朝}}, under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.|ja|セソストリスIII}}, the designation ''Day of the Sand Lifting of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス}} on his mountain in the pyramid city of Sesostris II, the Blessed'' was also used. The {{ill|Sesostris II Pyramid|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|ja|セソストリスIIのピラミッド}} of {{ill|Al-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|ja|アル・ラフーン}} was traditionally assigned, by virtue of its location, to the ''Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス}}''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|セソストリスIIの葬祭寺}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリンパピルス10248}}) it is recorded in the 14th regnal year of Sesostris III that the announcement in the {{ill|Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦}} for {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アチェット}} on the second lunar day was made. Since in the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|アクロバット団の祭日暦}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhet III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメネムヘトIII}} the Menchet Festival for the second month Achet was scheduled without a specified date, an announcement of the lunar date was not yet available at the time of the creation of that festival calendar. ==Literature== * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|ja|ライナー・ハンニグ}}: ''Great Hand Dictionary of Egyptian – German. (2800 – 950 BC). The Language of the Pharaohs'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = {{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史}}. Volume 64). Marburg Edition, 4th revised edition, by Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Dates. Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt'' (= ''Hildesheim Egyptological Contributions (HÄB)''. Volume 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Incorrect ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Memoirs of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class (AM-GS)''. 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz et al. 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Festival Calendar of the Month Achet II]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Chenep-scha]] [[Category:qq]] [[de:Chenep-scha]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[sn:Chenep-scha]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Chenep-scha]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:Chenep-scha]]

''Festival of Sand Shifting / Sand Lifting''

Dynastie|ja|第12王朝}}, under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.|ja|セソストリスIII}}, the designation ''Day of the Sand Lifting of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス}} on his mountain in the pyramid city of Sesostris II, the Blessed'' was also used. The {{ill|Sesostris II Pyramid|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|ja|セソストリスIIのピラミッド}} of {{ill|Al-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|ja|アル・ラフーン}} was traditionally assigned, by virtue of its location, to the ''Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス}}''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|セソストリスIIの葬祭寺}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリンパピルス10248}}) it is recorded in the 14th regnal year of Sesostris III that the announcement in the {{ill|Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦}} for {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アチェット}} on the second lunar day was made. Since in the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|アクロバット団の祭日暦}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhet III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメネムヘトIII}} the Menchet Festival for the second month Achet was scheduled without a specified date, an announcement of the lunar date was not yet available at the time of the creation of that festival calendar. ==Literature== * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|ja|ライナー・ハンニグ}}: ''Great Hand Dictionary of Egyptian – German. (2800 – 950 BC). The Language of the Pharaohs'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = {{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史}}. Volume 64). Marburg Edition, 4th revised edition, by Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Dates. Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt'' (= ''Hildesheim Egyptological Contributions (HÄB)''. Volume 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Incorrect ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Memoirs of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class (AM-GS)''. 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). ''Festival of Sand Shifting / Sand Lifting''
''Festival of Sand Dispersion'' |EXTENDED NAME2 = '''Festival Date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION = ''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second day of the moon in the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Sesostris II Pyramid in Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also known as '''Day of Sand Lifting''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国}} under the reign of King ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ}}) {{ill|Niuserre|de|Niuserre|ja|ニウセルレ}}. It is associated with the birth of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス}} and the goddess {{ill|Wosret|de|Wosret|ja|ウォスレット}}, attested in the {{ill|Middle Kingdom|de|Mittleres Reich|ja|エジプト中王国}} at {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|de|Theben (Ägypten)|ja|テーベ (エジプト)}}, who was venerated as the protector of the youthful Horus. ==Background== ===Old Kingdom=== The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the otherwise customary first day of the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月}}, but instead included the {{ill|Ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式}} of the ''Filling of the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Horusauge|ja|ホルスの目}}'' on the second day of the lunar month. The {{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭}} followed one day later on the third day and symbolized the ''Dressing of the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月}}''. ===12th Dynasty=== In the {{ill|12th Dynasty|de|12. Dynastie|ja|第12王朝}}, under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.|ja|セソストリスIII}}, the designation ''Day of the Sand Lifting of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス}} on his mountain in the pyramid city of Sesostris II, the Blessed'' was also used. The {{ill|Sesostris II Pyramid|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|ja|セソストリスIIのピラミッド}} of {{ill|Al-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|ja|アル・ラフーン}} was traditionally assigned, by virtue of its location, to the ''Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス}}''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|セソストリスIIの葬祭寺}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリンパピルス10248}}) it is recorded in the 14th regnal year of Sesostris III that the announcement in the {{ill|Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦}} for {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アチェット}} on the second lunar day was made. Since in the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|アクロバット団の祭日暦}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhet III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメネムヘトIII}} the Menchet Festival for the second month Achet was scheduled without a specified date, an announcement of the lunar date was not yet available at the time of the creation of that festival calendar. ==Literature== * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|ja|ライナー・ハンニグ}}: ''Great Hand Dictionary of Egyptian – German. (2800 – 950 BC). The Language of the Pharaohs'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = {{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史}}. Volume 64). Marburg Edition, 4th revised edition, by Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Dates. Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt'' (= ''Hildesheim Egyptological Contributions (HÄB)''. Volume 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Incorrect ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Memoirs of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class (AM-GS)''. 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz et al. 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Festival Calendar of the Month Achet II]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Chenep-scha]] [[de:Chenep-scha]] [[ja:Chenep-scha]]

Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Sand Shifting / Sand Lifting''
''Festival of Sand Dispersion'' |EXTENDED NAME2 = '''Festival Date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION = ''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second day of the moon in the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Sesostris II Pyramid in Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also known as '''Day of Sand Lifting''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国}} under the reign of King ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ}}) {{ill|Niuserre|de|Niuserre|ja|ニウセルレ}}. It is associated with the birth of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス}} and the goddess {{ill|Wosret|de|Wosret|ja|ウォスレット}}, attested in the {{ill|Middle Kingdom|de|Mittleres Reich|ja|エジプト中王国}} at {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|de|Theben (Ägypten)|ja|テーベ (エジプト)}}, who was venerated as the protector of the youthful Horus. ==Background== ===Old Kingdom=== The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the otherwise customary first day of the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月}}, but instead included the {{ill|Ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式}} of the ''Filling of the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Horusauge|ja|ホルスの目}}'' on the second day of the lunar month. The {{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭}} followed one day later on the third day and symbolized the ''Dressing of the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月}}''. ===12th Dynasty=== In the {{ill|12th Dynasty|de|12. Dynastie|ja|第12王朝}}, under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.|ja|セソストリスIII}}, the designation ''Day of the Sand Lifting of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス}} on his mountain in the pyramid city of Sesostris II, the Blessed'' was also used. The {{ill|Sesostris II Pyramid|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|ja|セソストリスIIのピラミッド}} of {{ill|Al-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|ja|アル・ラフーン}} was traditionally assigned, by virtue of its location, to the ''Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス}}''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|セソストリスIIの葬祭寺}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリンパピルス10248}}) it is recorded in the 14th regnal year of Sesostris III that the announcement in the {{ill|Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦}} for {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アチェット}} on the second lunar day was made. Since in the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|アクロバット団の祭日暦}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhet III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメネムヘトIII}} the Menchet Festival for the second month Achet was scheduled without a specified date, an announcement of the lunar date was not yet available at the time of the creation of that festival calendar. ==Literature== * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|ja|ライナー・ハンニグ}}: ''Great Hand Dictionary of Egyptian – German. (2800 – 950 BC). The Language of the Pharaohs'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = {{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史}}. Volume 64). Marburg Edition, 4th revised edition, by Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Dates. (2800 – 950 BC). The Language of the Pharaohs'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = {{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史|12=draft}}. Volume 64). Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt'' (= ''Hildesheim Egyptological Contributions (HÄB)''. Volume 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Incorrect ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|12=draft}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Memoirs of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class (AM-GS)''. 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). '''{{PAGENAME}}''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview==
==References==
{{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Egyptian festival pages to be written]] [[Category:pages to be translated]] [[Category:pages created from Interlanguage links]] [[Category:ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Day of the Sand Holes]] [[de:Chenep-scha|12=draft]] [[sn:Day of the Sand Holes]]

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Revise|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节}}) The article currently has no inline references.}} {{Infobox Hieroglyphs Double Column |EXPANDED-NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Shifting Sand / Collecting Sand''
''Festival of Pouring Sand'' |EXPANDED-NAME2 = '''Festival date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION =''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second lunar day of the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Pyramid of Senusret II at Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also '''Day of Collecting Sand''') is documented from the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国}} under the reign of King ([[Pharaoh]]) {{ill|Nyuserre Ini|de|Niuserre|ja|ニウセルラー|zh|纽塞拉}}. It is associated with the birth of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯}} and the goddess {{ill|Wosret|de|Wosret|ja|ウォスレト|zh|沃斯雷特}}, attested in {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich|ja|中王国時代|zh|埃及中王国}} at {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|de|Theben (Ägypten)|ja|テーベ (エジプト)|zh|底比斯}}, who was revered as a protector of the youthful Horus. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the commonly observed first day of the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|zh|新月}}, but involved the {{ill|ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式|zh|仪式}} of ''filling the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Horusauge|ja|ホルスの目|zh|荷鲁斯之眼}}'' on the second lunar day. The {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节}} followed one day later on the third day, symbolizing the ''clothing of the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|zh|月亮}}''. === 12th Dynasty === In the {{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|ja|エジプト第12王朝|zh|埃及第十二王朝}}, under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|ja|センウセレト3世|zh|辛努塞尔特三世}}, the festival was also named ''Day of Collecting Sand of Anubis on his mountain in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed''. The {{ill|Pyramid of Senusret II|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|ja|センウセレト2世のピラミッド|zh|辛努塞尔特二世金字塔}} at {{ill|El Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|ja|エル・ラフーン|zh|拉洪}} was traditionally assigned to the ''Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|zh|阿努比斯}}''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary temple of Senusret II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|センウセレト2世葬祭殿|zh|辛努塞尔特二世的太庙}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリン・パピルス10248|zh|柏林纸草文献10248}}), the announcement is noted in the {{ill|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|古埃及阴历}} for the 14th regnal year of Senusret III on the {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アケト|zh|阿赫特}} 18, second lunar day. Since the {{ill|Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|曲芸団の祭典暦|zh|杂技团节日历}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメンエムハト3世|zh|阿蒙涅姆赫特三世}} scheduled the Menchet festival for the second month of Achet without specifying the date, a lunar date announcement had not been available at the time of creating the festival calendar. == Literature == * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|ja|ライナー・ハニヒ|zh|赖纳·汉尼格}}: ''Großes Handwörterbuch Ägyptisch – Deutsch. (2800 – 950 v. Chr.). Die Sprache der Pharaonen'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = ''{{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史|zh|古代世界文化史}}.'' Volume 64). Marburger Edition, 4th revised edition, von Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|zh|罗尔夫·克劳斯}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer ägyptologische Beiträge. (HÄB).'' Volume 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Incorrect ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz et al., 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} [[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Festival calendar of month Achet II]][[Category:translated pages]][[ja:砂を取る祭]][[sn:Chenep-scha]] ==References== {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Egyptian festival pages to be written]] [[Category:pages to be translated]] [[Category:pages created from Interlanguage links]] [[Category:ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Day of the Sand Holes]] [[de:Chenep-scha|12=draft]] [[sn:Day of the Sand Holes]]

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Revise|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节}}) The article currently has no inline references.}} {{Infobox Hieroglyphs Double Column |EXPANDED-NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Shifting Sand / Collecting Sand''
''Festival of Pouring Sand'' |EXPANDED-NAME2 = '''Festival date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION =''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second lunar day of the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Pyramid of Senusret II at Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also '''Day of Collecting Sand''') is documented from the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国}} under the reign of King ([[Pharaoh]]) {{ill|Nyuserre Ini|de|Niuserre|ja|ニウセルラー|zh|纽塞拉}}. It is associated with the birth of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯}} and the goddess {{ill|Wosret|de|Wosret|ja|ウォスレト|zh|沃斯雷特}}, attested in {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich|ja|中王国時代|zh|埃及中王国}} at {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|de|Theben (Ägypten)|ja|テーベ (エジプト)|zh|底比斯}}, who was revered as a protector of the youthful Horus. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the commonly observed first day of the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|zh|新月}}, but involved the {{ill|ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式|zh|仪式}} of ''filling the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Horusauge|ja|ホルスの目|zh|荷鲁斯之眼}}'' on the second lunar day. The {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节}} followed one day later on the third day, symbolizing the ''clothing of the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|zh|月亮}}''. === 12th Dynasty === In the {{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|ja|エジプト第12王朝|zh|埃及第十二王朝}}, under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|ja|センウセレト3世|zh|辛努塞尔特三世}}, the festival was also named ''Day of Collecting Sand of Anubis on his mountain in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed''. The {{ill|Pyramid of Senusret II|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|ja|センウセレト2世のピラミッド|zh|辛努塞尔特二世金字塔}} at {{ill|El Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|ja|エル・ラフーン|zh|拉洪}} was traditionally assigned to the ''Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|zh|阿努比斯}}''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary temple of Senusret II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|センウセレト2世葬祭殿|zh|辛努塞尔特二世的太庙}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリン・パピルス10248|zh|柏林纸草文献10248}}), the announcement is noted in the {{ill|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|古埃及阴历}} for the 14th regnal year of Senusret III on the {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アケト|zh|阿赫特}} 18, second lunar day. Since the {{ill|Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|曲芸団の祭典暦|zh|杂技团节日历}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメンエムハト3世|zh|阿蒙涅姆赫特三世}} scheduled the Menchet festival for the second month of Achet without specifying the date, a lunar date announcement had not been available at the time of creating the festival calendar. ==Overview 2== {{Improve||This article|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Discussion: Menchet-Fest|de|Diskussion: Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭の議論|12=draft}}) The article does not yet contain any individual citation.}} {{Infobox Hieroglyphs Two-Column |EXTENDED NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国|12=draft}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Sand Shifting / Sand Lifting''
''Festival of Sand Dispersion'' |EXTENDED NAME2 = '''Festival Date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION = ''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second day of the moon in the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Sesostris II Pyramid in Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also known as '''Day of Sand Lifting''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国|12=draft}} under the reign of King ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|12=draft}}) {{ill|Niuserre|de|Niuserre|ja|ニウセルレ|12=draft}}. It is associated with the birth of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|12=draft}} and the goddess {{ill|Wosret|de|Wosret|ja|ウォスレット|12=draft}}, attested in the {{ill|Middle Kingdom|de|Mittleres Reich|ja|エジプト中王国|12=draft}} at {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|de|Theben (Ägypten)|ja|テーベ (エジプト)|12=draft}}, who was venerated as the protector of the youthful Horus. ==Background== ===Old Kingdom=== The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the otherwise customary first day of the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|12=draft}}, but instead included the {{ill|Ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式|12=draft}} of the ''Filling of the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Horusauge|ja|ホルスの目|12=draft}}'' on the second day of the lunar month. The {{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭|12=draft}} followed one day later on the third day and symbolized the ''Dressing of the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|12=draft}}''. ===12th Dynasty=== In the {{ill|12th Dynasty|de|12. Dynastie|ja|第12王朝|12=draft}}, under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.|ja|セソストリスIII|12=draft}}, the designation ''Day of the Sand Lifting of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|12=draft}} on his mountain in the pyramid city of Sesostris II, the Blessed'' was also used. The {{ill|Sesostris II Pyramid|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|ja|セソストリスIIのピラミッド|12=draft}} of {{ill|Al-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|ja|アル・ラフーン|12=draft}} was traditionally assigned, by virtue of its location, to the ''Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|12=draft}}''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|セソストリスIIの葬祭寺|12=draft}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリンパピルス10248|12=draft}}) it is recorded in the 14th regnal year of Sesostris III that the announcement in the {{ill|Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|12=draft}} for {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アチェット|12=draft}} on the second lunar day was made. Since in the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|アクロバット団の祭日暦|12=draft}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhet III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメネムヘトIII|12=draft}} the Menchet Festival for the second month Achet was scheduled without a specified date, an announcement of the lunar date was not yet available at the time of the creation of that festival calendar. ==Literature== * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|ja|ライナー・ハンニグ|12=draft}}: ''Great Hand Dictionary of Egyptian – German. (2800 – 950 BC). The Language of the Pharaohs'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = {{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史|12=draft}}. Volume 64). Marburg Edition, 4th revised edition, by Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|12=draft}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Dates. Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt'' (= ''Hildesheim Egyptological Contributions (HÄB)''. Volume 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Incorrect ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|12=draft}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Memoirs of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class (AM-GS)''. 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz et al. 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Festival Calendar of the Month Achet II]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Chenep-scha]] [[de:Chenep-scha]] [[ja:Chenep-scha]] == Literature == * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|ja|ライナー・ハニヒ|zh|赖纳·汉尼格}}: ''Großes Handwörterbuch Ägyptisch – Deutsch. (2800 – 950 v. Chr.). Die Sprache der Pharaonen'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = ''{{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史|zh|古代世界文化史}}.'' Volume 64). Marburger Edition, 4th revised edition, von Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|zh|罗尔夫·克劳斯}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer ägyptologische Beiträge. (HÄB).'' Volume 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Incorrect ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz et al., 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} [[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Festival calendar of month Achet II]][[Category:translated pages]][[ja:砂を取る祭]][[sn:Chenep-scha]] ==References== {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Egyptian festival pages to be written]] [[Category:pages to be translated]] [[Category:pages created from Interlanguage links]] [[Category:ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Day of the Sand Holes]] [[de:Chenep-scha|12=draft]] [[sn:Day of the Sand Holes]]

From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary temple of Senusret II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|センウセレト2世葬祭殿|zh|辛努塞尔特二世的太庙}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリン・パピルス10248|zh|柏林纸草文献10248}}), the announcement is noted in the {{ill|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|古埃及阴历}} for the 14th regnal year of Senusret III on the {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アケト|zh|阿赫特}} 18, second lunar day. Since the {{ill|Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|曲芸団の祭典暦|zh|杂技团节日历}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメンエムハト3世|zh|阿蒙涅姆赫特三世}} scheduled the Menchet festival for the second month of Achet without specifying the date, a lunar date announcement had not been available at the time of creating the festival calendar. ==Overview 2== {{Improve||This article|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Discussion: Menchet-Fest|de|Diskussion: Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭の議論|12=draft}}) The article does not yet contain any individual citation.}} {{Infobox Hieroglyphs Two-Column |EXTENDED NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国|12=draft}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Sand Shifting / Sand Lifting''
''Festival of Sand Dispersion'' |EXTENDED NAME2 = '''Festival Date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION = ''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second day of the moon in the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Sesostris II Pyramid in Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also known as '''Day of Sand Lifting''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国|12=draft}} under the reign of King ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|12=draft}}) {{ill|Niuserre|de|Niuserre|ja|ニウセルレ|12=draft}}. It is associated with the birth of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|12=draft}} and the goddess {{ill|Wosret|de|Wosret|ja|ウォスレット|12=draft}}, attested in the {{ill|Middle Kingdom|de|Mittleres Reich|ja|エジプト中王国|12=draft}} at {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|de|Theben (Ägypten)|ja|テーベ (エジプト)|12=draft}}, who was venerated as the protector of the youthful Horus. ==Background== ===Old Kingdom=== The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the otherwise customary first day of the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|12=draft}}, but instead included the {{ill|Ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式|12=draft}} of the ''Filling of the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Horusauge|ja|ホルスの目|12=draft}}'' on the second day of the lunar month. The {{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭|12=draft}} followed one day later on the third day and symbolized the ''Dressing of the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|12=draft}}''. ===12th Dynasty=== In the {{ill|12th Dynasty|de|12. Dynastie|ja|第12王朝|12=draft}}, under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.|ja|セソストリスIII|12=draft}}, the designation ''Day of the Sand Lifting of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|12=draft}} on his mountain in the pyramid city of Sesostris II, the Blessed'' was also used. The {{ill|Sesostris II Pyramid|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|ja|セソストリスIIのピラミッド|12=draft}} of {{ill|Al-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|ja|アル・ラフーン|12=draft}} was traditionally assigned, by virtue of its location, to the ''Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|12=draft}}''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|セソストリスIIの葬祭寺|12=draft}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリンパピルス10248|12=draft}}) it is recorded in the 14th regnal year of Sesostris III that the announcement in the {{ill|Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|12=draft}} for {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アチェット|12=draft}} on the second lunar day was made. Since in the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|アクロバット団の祭日暦|12=draft}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhet III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメネムヘトIII|12=draft}} the Menchet Festival for the second month Achet was scheduled without a specified date, an announcement of the lunar date was not yet available at the time of the creation of that festival calendar. ==Literature== * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|ja|ライナー・ハンニグ|12=draft}}: ''Great Hand Dictionary of Egyptian – German. (2800 – 950 BC). The Language of the Pharaohs'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = {{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史|12=draft}}. Volume 64). Marburg Edition, 4th revised edition, by Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|12=draft}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Dates. Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt'' (= ''Hildesheim Egyptological Contributions (HÄB)''. Volume 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Incorrect ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|12=draft}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Memoirs of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class (AM-GS)''. 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz et al. 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Festival Calendar of the Month Achet II]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Chenep-scha]] [[de:Chenep-scha]] [[ja:Chenep-scha]] == Literature == * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|ja|ライナー・ハニヒ|zh|赖纳·汉尼格}}: ''Großes Handwörterbuch Ägyptisch – Deutsch. (2800 – 950 v. Chr.). Die Sprache der Pharaonen'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = ''{{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史|zh|古代世界文化史}}.'' Volume 64). Marburger Edition, 4th revised edition, von Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|zh|罗尔夫·克劳斯}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer ägyptologische Beiträge. (HÄB).'' Volume 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Incorrect ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz et al., 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} [[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Festival calendar of month Achet II]][[Category:translated pages]][[ja:砂を取る祭]][[sn:Chenep-scha]] ==References== {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:pages to be translated]] [[Category:pages created from Interlanguage links]] [[Category:ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Day of the Sand Holes]] [[de:Chenep-scha|12=draft]] [[sn:Day of the Sand Holes]]

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Revise|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节}}) The article currently has no inline references.}} {{Infobox Hieroglyphs Double Column |EXPANDED-NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Shifting Sand / Collecting Sand''
''Festival of Pouring Sand'' |EXPANDED-NAME2 = '''Festival date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION =''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second lunar day of the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Pyramid of Senusret II at Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also '''Day of Collecting Sand''') is documented from the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国}} under the reign of King ([[Pharaoh]]) {{ill|Nyuserre Ini|de|Niuserre|ja|ニウセルラー|zh|纽塞拉}}. It is associated with the birth of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯}} and the goddess {{ill|Wosret|de|Wosret|ja|ウォスレト|zh|沃斯雷特}}, attested in {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich|ja|中王国時代|zh|埃及中王国}} at {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|de|Theben (Ägypten)|ja|テーベ (エジプト)|zh|底比斯}}, who was revered as a protector of the youthful Horus. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the commonly observed first day of the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|zh|新月}}, but involved the {{ill|ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式|zh|仪式}} of ''filling the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Horusauge|ja|ホルスの目|zh|荷鲁斯之眼}}'' on the second lunar day. The {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节}} followed one day later on the third day, symbolizing the ''clothing of the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|zh|月亮}}''. === 12th Dynasty === In the {{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|ja|エジプト第12王朝|zh|埃及第十二王朝}}, under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|ja|センウセレト3世|zh|辛努塞尔特三世}}, the festival was also named ''Day of Collecting Sand of Anubis on his mountain in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed''. The {{ill|Pyramid of Senusret II|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|ja|センウセレト2世のピラミッド|zh|辛努塞尔特二世金字塔}} at {{ill|El Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|ja|エル・ラフーン|zh|拉洪}} was traditionally assigned to the ''Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|zh|阿努比斯}}''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary temple of Senusret II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|センウセレト2世葬祭殿|zh|辛努塞尔特二世的太庙}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリン・パピルス10248|zh|柏林纸草文献10248}}), the announcement is noted in the {{ill|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|古埃及阴历}} for the 14th regnal year of Senusret III on the {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アケト|zh|阿赫特}} 18, second lunar day. Since the {{ill|Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|曲芸団の祭典暦|zh|杂技团节日历}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメンエムハト3世|zh|阿蒙涅姆赫特三世}} scheduled the Menchet festival for the second month of Achet without specifying the date, a lunar date announcement had not been available at the time of creating the festival calendar. ==Overview 2== {{Improve||This article|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Discussion: Menchet-Fest|de|Diskussion: Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭の議論|12=draft}}) The article does not yet contain any individual citation.}} {{Infobox Hieroglyphs Two-Column |EXTENDED NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国|12=draft}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Sand Shifting / Sand Lifting''
''Festival of Sand Dispersion'' |EXTENDED NAME2 = '''Festival Date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION = ''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second day of the moon in the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Sesostris II Pyramid in Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also known as '''Day of Sand Lifting''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国|12=draft}} under the reign of King ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|12=draft}}) {{ill|Niuserre|de|Niuserre|ja|ニウセルレ|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Pyramid of Senusret II|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|ja|センウセレト2世のピラミッド|zh|辛努塞尔特二世金字塔}} at {{ill|El Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|ja|エル・ラフーン|zh|拉洪}} was traditionally assigned to the ''Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|zh|阿努比斯}}''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary temple of Senusret II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|センウセレト2世葬祭殿|zh|辛努塞尔特二世的太庙}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリン・パピルス10248|zh|柏林纸草文献10248}}), the announcement is noted in the {{ill|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|古埃及阴历}} for the 14th regnal year of Senusret III on the {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アケト|zh|阿赫特}} 18, second lunar day. Since the {{ill|Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|曲芸団の祭典暦|zh|杂技团节日历}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメンエムハト3世|zh|阿蒙涅姆赫特三世}} scheduled the Menchet festival for the second month of Achet without specifying the date, a lunar date announcement had not been available at the time of creating the festival calendar. ==Overview 2== {{Improve||This article|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Discussion: Menchet-Fest|de|Diskussion: Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭の議論|12=draft}}) The article does not yet contain any individual citation.}} {{Infobox Hieroglyphs Two-Column |EXTENDED NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国|12=draft}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Sand Shifting / Sand Lifting''
''Festival of Sand Dispersion'' |EXTENDED NAME2 = '''Festival Date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION = ''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second day of the moon in the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Sesostris II Pyramid in Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also known as '''Day of Sand Lifting''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国|12=draft}} under the reign of King ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|12=draft}}) {{ill|Niuserre|de|Niuserre|ja|ニウセルレ|12=draft}}. It is associated with the birth of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|12=draft}} and the goddess {{ill|Wosret|de|Wosret|ja|ウォスレット|12=draft}}, attested in the {{ill|Middle Kingdom|de|Mittleres Reich|ja|エジプト中王国|12=draft}} at {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|de|Theben (Ägypten)|ja|テーベ (エジプト)|12=draft}}, who was venerated as the protector of the youthful Horus. ==Background== ===Old Kingdom=== The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the otherwise customary first day of the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|12=draft}}, but instead included the {{ill|Ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式|12=draft}} of the ''Filling of the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Horusauge|ja|ホルスの目|12=draft}}'' on the second day of the lunar month. The {{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭|12=draft}} followed one day later on the third day and symbolized the ''Dressing of the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|12=draft}}''. ===12th Dynasty=== In the {{ill|12th Dynasty|de|12. Dynastie|ja|第12王朝|12=draft}}, under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.|ja|セソストリスIII|12=draft}}, the designation ''Day of the Sand Lifting of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|12=draft}} on his mountain in the pyramid city of Sesostris II, the Blessed'' was also used. The {{ill|Sesostris II Pyramid|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|ja|セソストリスIIのピラミッド|12=draft}} of {{ill|Al-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|ja|アル・ラフーン|12=draft}} was traditionally assigned, by virtue of its location, to the ''Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|12=draft}}''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|セソストリスIIの葬祭寺|12=draft}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリンパピルス10248|12=draft}}) it is recorded in the 14th regnal year of Sesostris III that the announcement in the {{ill|Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|12=draft}} for {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アチェット|12=draft}} on the second lunar day was made. Since in the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|アクロバット団の祭日暦|12=draft}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhet III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメネムヘトIII|12=draft}} the Menchet Festival for the second month Achet was scheduled without a specified date, an announcement of the lunar date was not yet available at the time of the creation of that festival calendar. ==Literature== * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|ja|ライナー・ハンニグ|12=draft}}: ''Great Hand Dictionary of Egyptian – German. (2800 – 950 BC). The Language of the Pharaohs'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = {{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史|12=draft}}. Volume 64). Marburg Edition, 4th revised edition, by Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|12=draft}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Dates. Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt'' (= ''Hildesheim Egyptological Contributions (HÄB)''. Volume 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Incorrect ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|12=draft}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Memoirs of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class (AM-GS)''. 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz et al. 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Festival Calendar of the Month Achet II]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Chenep-scha]] [[de:Chenep-scha]] [[ja:Chenep-scha]] == Literature == * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|ja|ライナー・ハニヒ|zh|赖纳·汉尼格}}: ''Großes Handwörterbuch Ägyptisch – Deutsch. (2800 – 950 v. Chr.). Die Sprache der Pharaonen'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = ''{{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史|zh|古代世界文化史}}.'' Volume 64). Marburger Edition, 4th revised edition, von Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|zh|罗尔夫·克劳斯}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer ägyptologische Beiträge. (HÄB).'' Volume 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Incorrect ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz et al., 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} [[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Festival calendar of month Achet II]][[ja:砂を取る祭]][[sn:Chenep-scha]] ==References== {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:pages created from Interlanguage links]] [[Category:ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Day of the Sand Holes]] [[de:Chenep-scha|12=draft]] [[sn:Day of the Sand Holes]]
