''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Shifting Sand / Collecting Sand''
''Festival of Pouring Sand'' |EXPANDED-NAME2 = '''Festival date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION =''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second lunar day of the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Pyramid of Senusret II at Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also '''Day of Collecting Sand''') is documented from the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国|12=draft}} under the reign of King ([[Pharaoh]]) {{ill|Nyuserre Ini|de|Niuserre|ja|ニウセルラー|zh|纽塞拉|12=draft}}. It is associated with the birth of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯|12=draft}} and the goddess {{ill|Wosret|de|Wosret|ja|ウォスレト|zh|沃斯雷特|12=draft}}, attested in {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich|ja|中王国時代|zh|埃及中王国|12=draft}} at {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|de|Theben (Ägypten)|ja|テーベ (エジプト)|zh|底比斯|12=draft}}, who was revered as a protector of the youthful Horus. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the commonly observed first day of the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|zh|新月|12=draft}}, but involved the {{ill|ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|12=draft}} of ''filling the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Horusauge|ja|ホルスの目|zh|荷鲁斯之眼|12=draft}}'' on the second lunar day. The {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节|12=draft}} followed one day later on the third day, symbolizing the ''clothing of the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|zh|月亮|12=draft}}''. === 12th Dynasty === In the {{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|ja|エジプト第12王朝|zh|埃及第十二王朝|12=draft}}, under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|ja|センウセレト3世|zh|辛努塞尔特三世|12=draft}}, the festival was also named ''Day of Collecting Sand of Anubis on his mountain in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed''. The {{ill|Pyramid of Senusret II|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|ja|センウセレト2世のピラミッド|zh|辛努塞尔特二世金字塔|12=draft}} at {{ill|El Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|ja|エル・ラフーン|zh|拉洪|12=draft}} was traditionally assigned to the ''Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|zh|阿努比斯|12=draft}}''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary temple of Senusret II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|センウセレト2世葬祭殿|zh|辛努塞尔特二世的太庙|12=draft}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリン・パピルス10248|zh|柏林纸草文献10248|12=draft}}), the announcement is noted in the {{ill|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|古埃及阴历|12=draft}} for the 14th regnal year of Senusret III on the {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アケト|zh|阿赫特|12=draft}} 18, second lunar day. Since the {{ill|Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|曲芸団の祭典暦|zh|杂技团节日历|12=draft}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメンエムハト3世|zh|阿蒙涅姆赫特三世|12=draft}} scheduled the Menchet festival for the second month of Achet without specifying the date, a lunar date announcement had not been available at the time of creating the festival calendar. ==Overview 2== {{Improve||This article|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Discussion: Menchet-Fest|de|Diskussion: Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭の議論|12=draft|12=draft}}) The article does not yet contain any individual citation.}} {{Infobox Hieroglyphs Two-Column |EXTENDED NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国|12=draft|12=draft}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2

It is associated with the birth of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|12=draft|12=draft}} and the goddess {{ill|Wosret|de|Wosret|ja|ウォスレット|12=draft|12=draft}}, attested in the {{ill|Middle Kingdom|de|Mittleres Reich|ja|エジプト中王国|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|de|Theben (Ägypten)|ja|テーベ (エジプト)|12=draft|12=draft}}, who was venerated as the protector of the youthful Horus. ==Background== ===Old Kingdom=== The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the otherwise customary first day of the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|12=draft|12=draft}}, but instead included the {{ill|Ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式|12=draft|12=draft}} of the ''Filling of the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Horusauge|ja|ホルスの目|12=draft|12=draft}}'' on the second day of the lunar month. The {{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭|12=draft|12=draft}} followed one day later on the third day and symbolized the ''Dressing of the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|12=draft|12=draft}}''. ===12th Dynasty=== In the {{ill|12th Dynasty|de|12. Dynastie|ja|第12王朝|12=draft|12=draft}}, under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.|ja|セソストリスIII|12=draft|12=draft}}, the designation ''Day of the Sand Lifting of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|12=draft|12=draft}} on his mountain in the pyramid city of Sesostris II, the Blessed'' was also used. The {{ill|Sesostris II Pyramid|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|ja|セソストリスIIのピラミッド|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Al-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|ja|アル・ラフーン|12=draft|12=draft}} was traditionally assigned, by virtue of its location, to the ''Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|12=draft|12=draft}}''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|セソストリスIIの葬祭寺|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリンパピルス10248|12=draft|12=draft}}) it is recorded in the 14th regnal year of Sesostris III that the announcement in the {{ill|Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|12=draft|12=draft}} for {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アチェット|12=draft|12=draft}} on the second lunar day was made. Since in the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|アクロバット団の祭日暦|12=draft|12=draft}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhet III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメネムヘトIII|12=draft|12=draft}} the Menchet Festival for the second month Achet was scheduled without a specified date, an announcement of the lunar date was not yet available at the time of the creation of that festival calendar. ==Literature== * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|ja|ライナー・ハンニグ|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Great Hand Dictionary of Egyptian – German. '''{{PAGENAME}}''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Revise|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节|12=draft}}) The article currently has no inline references.}} {{Infobox Hieroglyphs Double Column |EXPANDED-NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国|12=draft}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Shifting Sand / Collecting Sand''
''Festival of Pouring Sand'' |EXPANDED-NAME2 = '''Festival date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION =''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second lunar day of the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Pyramid of Senusret II at Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also '''Day of Collecting Sand''') is documented from the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国|12=draft}} under the reign of King ([[Pharaoh]]) {{ill|Nyuserre Ini|de|Niuserre|ja|ニウセルラー|zh|纽塞拉|12=draft}}. It is associated with the birth of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯|12=draft}} and the goddess {{ill|Wosret|de|Wosret|ja|ウォスレト|zh|沃斯雷特|12=draft}}, attested in {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich|ja|中王国時代|zh|埃及中王国|12=draft}} at {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|de|Theben (Ägypten)|ja|テーベ (エジプト)|zh|底比斯|12=draft}}, who was revered as a protector of the youthful Horus. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the commonly observed first day of the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|zh|新月|12=draft}}, but involved the {{ill|ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|12=draft}} of ''filling the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Horusauge|ja|ホルスの目|zh|荷鲁斯之眼|12=draft}}'' on the second lunar day. The {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节|12=draft}} followed one day later on the third day, symbolizing the ''clothing of the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|zh|月亮|12=draft}}''. === 12th Dynasty === In the {{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|ja|エジプト第12王朝|zh|埃及第十二王朝|12=draft}}, under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|ja|センウセレト3世|zh|辛努塞尔特三世|12=draft}}, the festival was also named ''Day of Collecting Sand of Anubis on his mountain in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed''. The {{ill|Pyramid of Senusret II|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|ja|センウセレト2世のピラミッド|zh|辛努塞尔特二世金字塔|12=draft}} at {{ill|El Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|ja|エル・ラフーン|zh|拉洪|12=draft}} was traditionally assigned to the ''Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|zh|阿努比斯|12=draft}}''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary temple of Senusret II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|センウセレト2世葬祭殿|zh|辛努塞尔特二世的太庙|12=draft}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリン・パピルス10248|zh|柏林纸草文献10248|12=draft}}), the announcement is noted in the {{ill|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|古埃及阴历|12=draft}} for the 14th regnal year of Senusret III on the {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アケト|zh|阿赫特|12=draft}} 18, second lunar day. Since the {{ill|Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|曲芸団の祭典暦|zh|杂技团节日历|12=draft}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメンエムハト3世|zh|阿蒙涅姆赫特三世|12=draft}} scheduled the Menchet festival for the second month of Achet without specifying the date, a lunar date announcement had not been available at the time of creating the festival calendar. ==Overview 2== {{Improve||This article|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Discussion: Menchet-Fest|de|Diskussion: Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭の議論|12=draft|12=draft}}) The article does not yet contain any individual citation.}} {{Infobox Hieroglyphs Two-Column |EXTENDED NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国|12=draft|12=draft}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Sand Shifting / Sand Lifting''
''Festival of Sand Dispersion'' |EXTENDED NAME2 = '''Festival Date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION = ''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second day of the moon in the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Sesostris II Pyramid in Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also known as '''Day of Sand Lifting''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国|12=draft|12=draft}} under the reign of King ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|12=draft|12=draft}}) {{ill|Niuserre|de|Niuserre|ja|ニウセルレ|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is associated with the birth of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|12=draft|12=draft}} and the goddess {{ill|Wosret|de|Wosret|ja|ウォスレット|12=draft|12=draft}}, attested in the {{ill|Middle Kingdom|de|Mittleres Reich|ja|エジプト中王国|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|de|Theben (Ägypten)|ja|テーベ (エジプト)|12=draft|12=draft}}, who was venerated as the protector of the youthful Horus. ==Background== ===Old Kingdom=== The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the otherwise customary first day of the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|12=draft|12=draft}}, but instead included the {{ill|Ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式|12=draft|12=draft}} of the ''Filling of the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Horusauge|ja|ホルスの目|12=draft|12=draft}}'' on the second day of the lunar month. The {{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭|12=draft|12=draft}} followed one day later on the third day and symbolized the ''Dressing of the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|12=draft|12=draft}}''. ===12th Dynasty=== In the {{ill|12th Dynasty|de|12. Dynastie|ja|第12王朝|12=draft|12=draft}}, under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.|ja|セソストリスIII|12=draft|12=draft}}, the designation ''Day of the Sand Lifting of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|12=draft|12=draft}} on his mountain in the pyramid city of Sesostris II, the Blessed'' was also used. The {{ill|Sesostris II Pyramid|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|ja|セソストリスIIのピラミッド|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Al-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|ja|アル・ラフーン|12=draft|12=draft}} was traditionally assigned, by virtue of its location, to the ''Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|12=draft|12=draft}}''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|セソストリスIIの葬祭寺|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリンパピルス10248|12=draft|12=draft}}) it is recorded in the 14th regnal year of Sesostris III that the announcement in the {{ill|Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|12=draft|12=draft}} for {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アチェット|12=draft|12=draft}} on the second lunar day was made. Since in the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|アクロバット団の祭日暦|12=draft|12=draft}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhet III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメネムヘトIII|12=draft|12=draft}} the Menchet Festival for the second month Achet was scheduled without a specified date, an announcement of the lunar date was not yet available at the time of the creation of that festival calendar. ==Literature== * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|ja|ライナー・ハンニグ|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Great Hand Dictionary of Egyptian – German. (2800 – 950 BC). The Language of the Pharaohs'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = {{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史|12=draft|12=draft}}. '''{{PAGENAME}}''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Revise|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节|12=draft}}) The article currently has no inline references.}} {{Infobox Hieroglyphs Double Column |EXPANDED-NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国|12=draft}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Shifting Sand / Collecting Sand''
''Festival of Pouring Sand'' |EXPANDED-NAME2 = '''Festival date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION =''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second lunar day of the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Pyramid of Senusret II at Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also '''Day of Collecting Sand''') is documented from the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国|12=draft}} under the reign of King ([[Pharaoh]]) {{ill|Nyuserre Ini|de|Niuserre|ja|ニウセルラー|zh|纽塞拉|12=draft}}. It is associated with the birth of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯|12=draft}} and the goddess {{ill|Wosret|de|Wosret|ja|ウォスレト|zh|沃斯雷特|12=draft}}, attested in {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich|ja|中王国時代|zh|埃及中王国|12=draft}} at {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|de|Theben (Ägypten)|ja|テーベ (エジプト)|zh|底比斯|12=draft}}, who was revered as a protector of the youthful Horus. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the commonly observed first day of the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|zh|新月|12=draft}}, but involved the {{ill|ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|12=draft}} of ''filling the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Horusauge|ja|ホルスの目|zh|荷鲁斯之眼|12=draft}}'' on the second lunar day. The {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节|12=draft}} followed one day later on the third day, symbolizing the ''clothing of the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|zh|月亮|12=draft}}''. === 12th Dynasty === In the {{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|ja|エジプト第12王朝|zh|埃及第十二王朝|12=draft}}, under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|ja|センウセレト3世|zh|辛努塞尔特三世|12=draft}}, the festival was also named ''Day of Collecting Sand of Anubis on his mountain in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed''. The {{ill|Pyramid of Senusret II|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|ja|センウセレト2世のピラミッド|zh|辛努塞尔特二世金字塔|12=draft}} at {{ill|El Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|ja|エル・ラフーン|zh|拉洪|12=draft}} was traditionally assigned to the ''Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|zh|阿努比斯|12=draft}}''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary temple of Senusret II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|センウセレト2世葬祭殿|zh|辛努塞尔特二世的太庙|12=draft}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリン・パピルス10248|zh|柏林纸草文献10248|12=draft}}), the announcement is noted in the {{ill|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|古埃及阴历|12=draft}} for the 14th regnal year of Senusret III on the {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アケト|zh|阿赫特|12=draft}} 18, second lunar day. Since the {{ill|Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|曲芸団の祭典暦|zh|杂技团节日历|12=draft}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメンエムハト3世|zh|阿蒙涅姆赫特三世|12=draft}} scheduled the Menchet festival for the second month of Achet without specifying the date, a lunar date announcement had not been available at the time of creating the festival calendar. ==Overview 2== {{Improve||This article|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Discussion: Menchet-Fest|de|Diskussion: Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭の議論|12=draft|12=draft}}) The article does not yet contain any individual citation.}} {{Infobox Hieroglyphs Two-Column |EXTENDED NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国|12=draft|12=draft}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Sand Shifting / Sand Lifting''
''Festival of Sand Dispersion'' |EXTENDED NAME2 = '''Festival Date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION = ''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second day of the moon in the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Sesostris II Pyramid in Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also known as '''Day of Sand Lifting''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国|12=draft|12=draft}} under the reign of King ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|12=draft|12=draft}}) {{ill|Niuserre|de|Niuserre|ja|ニウセルレ|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is associated with the birth of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|12=draft|12=draft}} and the goddess {{ill|Wosret|de|Wosret|ja|ウォスレット|12=draft|12=draft}}, attested in the {{ill|Middle Kingdom|de|Mittleres Reich|ja|エジプト中王国|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|de|Theben (Ägypten)|ja|テーベ (エジプト)|12=draft|12=draft}}, who was venerated as the protector of the youthful Horus. ==Background== ===Old Kingdom=== The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the otherwise customary first day of the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|12=draft|12=draft}}, but instead included the {{ill|Ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式|12=draft|12=draft}} of the ''Filling of the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Horusauge|ja|ホルスの目|12=draft|12=draft}}'' on the second day of the lunar month. The {{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭|12=draft|12=draft}} followed one day later on the third day and symbolized the ''Dressing of the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|12=draft|12=draft}}''. ===12th Dynasty=== In the {{ill|12th Dynasty|de|12. Dynastie|ja|第12王朝|12=draft|12=draft}}, under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.|ja|セソストリスIII|12=draft|12=draft}}, the designation ''Day of the Sand Lifting of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|12=draft|12=draft}} on his mountain in the pyramid city of Sesostris II, the Blessed'' was also used. The {{ill|Sesostris II Pyramid|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|ja|セソストリスIIのピラミッド|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Al-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|ja|アル・ラフーン|12=draft|12=draft}} was traditionally assigned, by virtue of its location, to the ''Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|12=draft|12=draft}}''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|セソストリスIIの葬祭寺|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリンパピルス10248|12=draft|12=draft}}) it is recorded in the 14th regnal year of Sesostris III that the announcement in the {{ill|Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|12=draft|12=draft}} for {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アチェット|12=draft|12=draft}} on the second lunar day was made. Since in the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|アクロバット団の祭日暦|12=draft|12=draft}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhet III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメネムヘトIII|12=draft|12=draft}} the Menchet Festival for the second month Achet was scheduled without a specified date, an announcement of the lunar date was not yet available at the time of the creation of that festival calendar. ==Literature== * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|ja|ライナー・ハンニグ|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Great Hand Dictionary of Egyptian – German. Marburger Edition, 4th revised edition, von Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|zh|罗尔夫·克劳斯|12=draft}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer ägyptologische Beiträge. (HÄB).'' Volume 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Incorrect ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|12=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). '''{{PAGENAME}}''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Revise|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节|12=draft}}) The article currently has no inline references.}} {{Infobox Hieroglyphs Double Column |EXPANDED-NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国|12=draft}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Shifting Sand / Collecting Sand''
''Festival of Pouring Sand'' |EXPANDED-NAME2 = '''Festival date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION =''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second lunar day of the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Pyramid of Senusret II at Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also '''Day of Collecting Sand''') is documented from the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国|12=draft}} under the reign of King ([[Pharaoh]]) {{ill|Nyuserre Ini|de|Niuserre|ja|ニウセルラー|zh|纽塞拉|12=draft}}. It is associated with the birth of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯|12=draft}} and the goddess {{ill|Wosret|de|Wosret|ja|ウォスレト|zh|沃斯雷特|12=draft}}, attested in {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich|ja|中王国時代|zh|埃及中王国|12=draft}} at {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|de|Theben (Ägypten)|ja|テーベ (エジプト)|zh|底比斯|12=draft}}, who was revered as a protector of the youthful Horus. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the commonly observed first day of the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|zh|新月|12=draft}}, but involved the {{ill|ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|12=draft}} of ''filling the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Horusauge|ja|ホルスの目|zh|荷鲁斯之眼|12=draft}}'' on the second lunar day. The {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节|12=draft}} followed one day later on the third day, symbolizing the ''clothing of the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|zh|月亮|12=draft}}''. === 12th Dynasty === In the {{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|ja|エジプト第12王朝|zh|埃及第十二王朝|12=draft}}, under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|ja|センウセレト3世|zh|辛努塞尔特三世|12=draft}}, the festival was also named ''Day of Collecting Sand of Anubis on his mountain in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed''. The {{ill|Pyramid of Senusret II|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|ja|センウセレト2世のピラミッド|zh|辛努塞尔特二世金字塔|12=draft}} at {{ill|El Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|ja|エル・ラフーン|zh|拉洪|12=draft}} was traditionally assigned to the ''Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|zh|阿努比斯|12=draft}}''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary temple of Senusret II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|センウセレト2世葬祭殿|zh|辛努塞尔特二世的太庙|12=draft}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリン・パピルス10248|zh|柏林纸草文献10248|12=draft}}), the announcement is noted in the {{ill|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|古埃及阴历|12=draft}} for the 14th regnal year of Senusret III on the {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アケト|zh|阿赫特|12=draft}} 18, second lunar day. Since the {{ill|Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|曲芸団の祭典暦|zh|杂技团节日历|12=draft}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメンエムハト3世|zh|阿蒙涅姆赫特三世|12=draft}} scheduled the Menchet festival for the second month of Achet without specifying the date, a lunar date announcement had not been available at the time of creating the festival calendar. ==Overview 2== {{Improve||This article|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Discussion: Menchet-Fest|de|Diskussion: Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭の議論|12=draft|12=draft}}) The article does not yet contain any individual citation.}} {{Infobox Hieroglyphs Two-Column |EXTENDED NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国|12=draft|12=draft}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Sand Shifting / Sand Lifting''
''Festival of Sand Dispersion'' |EXTENDED NAME2 = '''Festival Date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION = ''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second day of the moon in the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Sesostris II Pyramid in Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also known as '''Day of Sand Lifting''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国|12=draft|12=draft}} under the reign of King ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|12=draft|12=draft}}) {{ill|Niuserre|de|Niuserre|ja|ニウセルレ|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is associated with the birth of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|12=draft|12=draft}} and the goddess {{ill|Wosret|de|Wosret|ja|ウォスレット|12=draft|12=draft}}, attested in the {{ill|Middle Kingdom|de|Mittleres Reich|ja|エジプト中王国|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|de|Theben (Ägypten)|ja|テーベ (エジプト)|12=draft|12=draft}}, who was venerated as the protector of the youthful Horus. ==Background== ===Old Kingdom=== The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the otherwise customary first day of the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|12=draft|12=draft}}, but instead included the {{ill|Ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式|12=draft|12=draft}} of the ''Filling of the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Horusauge|ja|ホルスの目|12=draft|12=draft}}'' on the second day of the lunar month. The {{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭|12=draft|12=draft}} followed one day later on the third day and symbolized the ''Dressing of the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|12=draft|12=draft}}''. ===12th Dynasty=== In the {{ill|12th Dynasty|de|12. Dynastie|ja|第12王朝|12=draft|12=draft}}, under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.|ja|セソストリスIII|12=draft|12=draft}}, the designation ''Day of the Sand Lifting of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|12=draft|12=draft}} on his mountain in the pyramid city of Sesostris II, the Blessed'' was also used. The {{ill|Sesostris II Pyramid|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|ja|セソストリスIIのピラミッド|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Al-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|ja|アル・ラフーン|12=draft|12=draft}} was traditionally assigned, by virtue of its location, to the ''Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|12=draft|12=draft}}''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|セソストリスIIの葬祭寺|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリンパピルス10248|12=draft|12=draft}}) it is recorded in the 14th regnal year of Sesostris III that the announcement in the {{ill|Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|12=draft|12=draft}} for {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アチェット|12=draft|12=draft}} on the second lunar day was made. Since in the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|アクロバット団の祭日暦|12=draft|12=draft}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhet III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメネムヘトIII|12=draft|12=draft}} the Menchet Festival for the second month Achet was scheduled without a specified date, an announcement of the lunar date was not yet available at the time of the creation of that festival calendar. ==Literature== * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|ja|ライナー・ハンニグ|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Great Hand Dictionary of Egyptian – German. (2800 – 950 BC). The Language of the Pharaohs'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = {{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史|12=draft|12=draft}}. Volume 64). Marburg Edition, 4th revised edition, by Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Dates. Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt'' (= ''Hildesheim Egyptological Contributions (HÄB)''. Volume 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Incorrect ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Memoirs of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class (AM-GS)''. 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz et al. 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} == Literature == * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|ja|ライナー・ハニヒ|zh|赖纳·汉尼格|12=draft}}: ''Großes Handwörterbuch Ägyptisch – Deutsch. (2800 – 950 v. Chr.). Die Sprache der Pharaonen'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = ''{{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史|zh|古代世界文化史|12=draft}}.'' Volume 64). Marburger Edition, 4th revised edition, von Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|zh|罗尔夫·克劳斯|12=draft}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer ägyptologische Beiträge. (HÄB).'' Volume 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Incorrect ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|12=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz et al., 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} ==References== {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories|[[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Festival Calendar of the Month Achet II]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Festival calendar of month Achet II]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] }} [[sn:Chenep-scha]] [[de:Chenep-scha]] [[ja:Chenep-scha]] [[ja:砂を取る祭]] [[sn:Day of the Sand Holes]] [[en:Day of the Sand Holes]] [[de:Chenep-scha|12=draft]]

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Revise|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节|12=draft}}) The article currently has no inline references.}} {{Infobox Hieroglyphs Double Column |EXPANDED-NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国|12=draft}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Shifting Sand / Collecting Sand''
''Festival of Pouring Sand'' |EXPANDED-NAME2 = '''Festival date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION =''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second lunar day of the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Pyramid of Senusret II at Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also '''Day of Collecting Sand''') is documented from the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国|12=draft}} under the reign of King ([[Pharaoh]]) {{ill|Nyuserre Ini|de|Niuserre|ja|ニウセルラー|zh|纽塞拉|12=draft}}. It is associated with the birth of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯|12=draft}} and the goddess {{ill|Wosret|de|Wosret|ja|ウォスレト|zh|沃斯雷特|12=draft}}, attested in {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich|ja|中王国時代|zh|埃及中王国|12=draft}} at {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|de|Theben (Ägypten)|ja|テーベ (エジプト)|zh|底比斯|12=draft}}, who was revered as a protector of the youthful Horus. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the commonly observed first day of the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|zh|新月|12=draft}}, but involved the {{ill|ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|12=draft}} of ''filling the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Horusauge|ja|ホルスの目|zh|荷鲁斯之眼|12=draft}}'' on the second lunar day. The {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节|12=draft}} followed one day later on the third day, symbolizing the ''clothing of the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|zh|月亮|12=draft}}''. === 12th Dynasty === In the {{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|ja|エジプト第12王朝|zh|埃及第十二王朝|12=draft}}, under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|ja|センウセレト3世|zh|辛努塞尔特三世|12=draft}}, the festival was also named ''Day of Collecting Sand of Anubis on his mountain in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed''. The {{ill|Pyramid of Senusret II|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|ja|センウセレト2世のピラミッド|zh|辛努塞尔特二世金字塔|12=draft}} at {{ill|El Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|ja|エル・ラフーン|zh|拉洪|12=draft}} was traditionally assigned to the ''Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|zh|阿努比斯|12=draft}}''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary temple of Senusret II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|センウセレト2世葬祭殿|zh|辛努塞尔特二世的太庙|12=draft}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリン・パピルス10248|zh|柏林纸草文献10248|12=draft}}), the announcement is noted in the {{ill|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|古埃及阴历|12=draft}} for the 14th regnal year of Senusret III on the {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アケト|zh|阿赫特|12=draft}} 18, second lunar day. Since the {{ill|Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|曲芸団の祭典暦|zh|杂技团节日历|12=draft}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメンエムハト3世|zh|阿蒙涅姆赫特三世|12=draft}} scheduled the Menchet festival for the second month of Achet without specifying the date, a lunar date announcement had not been available at the time of creating the festival calendar. ==Overview 2== {{Improve||This article|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Discussion: Menchet-Fest|de|Diskussion: Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭の議論|12=draft|12=draft}}) The article does not yet contain any individual citation.}} {{Infobox Hieroglyphs Two-Column |EXTENDED NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国|12=draft|12=draft}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Sand Shifting / Sand Lifting''
''Festival of Sand Dispersion'' |EXTENDED NAME2 = '''Festival Date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION = ''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second day of the moon in the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Sesostris II Pyramid in Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also known as '''Day of Sand Lifting''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国|12=draft|12=draft}} under the reign of King ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|12=draft|12=draft}}) {{ill|Niuserre|de|Niuserre|ja|ニウセルレ|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is associated with the birth of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|12=draft|12=draft}} and the goddess {{ill|Wosret|de|Wosret|ja|ウォスレット|12=draft|12=draft}}, attested in the {{ill|Middle Kingdom|de|Mittleres Reich|ja|エジプト中王国|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|de|Theben (Ägypten)|ja|テーベ (エジプト)|12=draft|12=draft}}, who was venerated as the protector of the youthful Horus. ==Background== ===Old Kingdom=== The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the otherwise customary first day of the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|12=draft|12=draft}}, but instead included the {{ill|Ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式|12=draft|12=draft}} of the ''Filling of the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Horusauge|ja|ホルスの目|12=draft|12=draft}}'' on the second day of the lunar month. The {{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭|12=draft|12=draft}} followed one day later on the third day and symbolized the ''Dressing of the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|12=draft|12=draft}}''. ===12th Dynasty=== In the {{ill|12th Dynasty|de|12. Dynastie|ja|第12王朝|12=draft|12=draft}}, under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.|ja|セソストリスIII|12=draft|12=draft}}, the designation ''Day of the Sand Lifting of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|12=draft|12=draft}} on his mountain in the pyramid city of Sesostris II, the Blessed'' was also used. The {{ill|Sesostris II Pyramid|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|ja|セソストリスIIのピラミッド|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Al-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|ja|アル・ラフーン|12=draft|12=draft}} was traditionally assigned, by virtue of its location, to the ''Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|12=draft|12=draft}}''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|セソストリスIIの葬祭寺|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリンパピルス10248|12=draft|12=draft}}) it is recorded in the 14th regnal year of Sesostris III that the announcement in the {{ill|Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|12=draft|12=draft}} for {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アチェット|12=draft|12=draft}} on the second lunar day was made. Since in the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|アクロバット団の祭日暦|12=draft|12=draft}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhet III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメネムヘトIII|12=draft|12=draft}} the Menchet Festival for the second month Achet was scheduled without a specified date, an announcement of the lunar date was not yet available at the time of the creation of that festival calendar. ==Literature== * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|ja|ライナー・ハンニグ|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Great Hand Dictionary of Egyptian – German. (2800 – 950 BC). The Language of the Pharaohs'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = {{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史|12=draft|12=draft}}. Volume 64). Marburg Edition, 4th revised edition, by Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Dates. Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt'' (= ''Hildesheim Egyptological Contributions (HÄB)''. Volume 20). Memoirs of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class (AM-GS)''. 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). '''{{PAGENAME}}''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Revise|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节|12=draft}}) The article currently has no inline references.}} {{Infobox Hieroglyphs Double Column |EXPANDED-NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国|12=draft}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Shifting Sand / Collecting Sand''
''Festival of Pouring Sand'' |EXPANDED-NAME2 = '''Festival date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION =''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second lunar day of the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Pyramid of Senusret II at Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also '''Day of Collecting Sand''') is documented from the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国|12=draft}} under the reign of King ([[Pharaoh]]) {{ill|Nyuserre Ini|de|Niuserre|ja|ニウセルラー|zh|纽塞拉|12=draft}}. It is associated with the birth of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯|12=draft}} and the goddess {{ill|Wosret|de|Wosret|ja|ウォスレト|zh|沃斯雷特|12=draft}}, attested in {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich|ja|中王国時代|zh|埃及中王国|12=draft}} at {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|de|Theben (Ägypten)|ja|テーベ (エジプト)|zh|底比斯|12=draft}}, who was revered as a protector of the youthful Horus. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the commonly observed first day of the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|zh|新月|12=draft}}, but involved the {{ill|ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|12=draft}} of ''filling the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Horusauge|ja|ホルスの目|zh|荷鲁斯之眼|12=draft}}'' on the second lunar day. The {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节|12=draft}} followed one day later on the third day, symbolizing the ''clothing of the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|zh|月亮|12=draft}}''. === 12th Dynasty === In the {{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|ja|エジプト第12王朝|zh|埃及第十二王朝|12=draft}}, under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|ja|センウセレト3世|zh|辛努塞尔特三世|12=draft}}, the festival was also named ''Day of Collecting Sand of Anubis on his mountain in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed''. The {{ill|Pyramid of Senusret II|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|ja|センウセレト2世のピラミッド|zh|辛努塞尔特二世金字塔|12=draft}} at {{ill|El Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|ja|エル・ラフーン|zh|拉洪|12=draft}} was traditionally assigned to the ''Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|zh|阿努比斯|12=draft}}''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary temple of Senusret II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|センウセレト2世葬祭殿|zh|辛努塞尔特二世的太庙|12=draft}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリン・パピルス10248|zh|柏林纸草文献10248|12=draft}}), the announcement is noted in the {{ill|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|古埃及阴历|12=draft}} for the 14th regnal year of Senusret III on the {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アケト|zh|阿赫特|12=draft}} 18, second lunar day. Since the {{ill|Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|曲芸団の祭典暦|zh|杂技团节日历|12=draft}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメンエムハト3世|zh|阿蒙涅姆赫特三世|12=draft}} scheduled the Menchet festival for the second month of Achet without specifying the date, a lunar date announcement had not been available at the time of creating the festival calendar. ==Overview 2== {{Improve||This article|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Discussion: Menchet-Fest|de|Diskussion: Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭の議論|12=draft|12=draft}}) The article does not yet contain any individual citation.}} {{Infobox Hieroglyphs Two-Column |EXTENDED NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国|12=draft|12=draft}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Sand Shifting / Sand Lifting''
''Festival of Sand Dispersion'' |EXTENDED NAME2 = '''Festival Date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION = ''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second day of the moon in the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Sesostris II Pyramid in Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also known as '''Day of Sand Lifting''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国|12=draft|12=draft}} under the reign of King ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|12=draft|12=draft}}) {{ill|Niuserre|de|Niuserre|ja|ニウセルレ|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is associated with the birth of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|12=draft|12=draft}} and the goddess {{ill|Wosret|de|Wosret|ja|ウォスレット|12=draft|12=draft}}, attested in the {{ill|Middle Kingdom|de|Mittleres Reich|ja|エジプト中王国|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|de|Theben (Ägypten)|ja|テーベ (エジプト)|12=draft|12=draft}}, who was venerated as the protector of the youthful Horus. ==Background== ===Old Kingdom=== The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the otherwise customary first day of the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|12=draft|12=draft}}, but instead included the {{ill|Ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式|12=draft|12=draft}} of the ''Filling of the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Horusauge|ja|ホルスの目|12=draft|12=draft}}'' on the second day of the lunar month. The {{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭|12=draft|12=draft}} followed one day later on the third day and symbolized the ''Dressing of the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|12=draft|12=draft}}''. ===12th Dynasty=== In the {{ill|12th Dynasty|de|12. Dynastie|ja|第12王朝|12=draft|12=draft}}, under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.|ja|セソストリスIII|12=draft|12=draft}}, the designation ''Day of the Sand Lifting of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|12=draft|12=draft}} on his mountain in the pyramid city of Sesostris II, the Blessed'' was also used. The {{ill|Sesostris II Pyramid|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|ja|セソストリスIIのピラミッド|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Al-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|ja|アル・ラフーン|12=draft|12=draft}} was traditionally assigned, by virtue of its location, to the ''Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|12=draft|12=draft}}''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|セソストリスIIの葬祭寺|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリンパピルス10248|12=draft|12=draft}}) it is recorded in the 14th regnal year of Sesostris III that the announcement in the {{ill|Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|12=draft|12=draft}} for {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アチェット|12=draft|12=draft}} on the second lunar day was made. Since in the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|アクロバット団の祭日暦|12=draft|12=draft}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhet III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメネムヘトIII|12=draft|12=draft}} the Menchet Festival for the second month Achet was scheduled without a specified date, an announcement of the lunar date was not yet available at the time of the creation of that festival calendar. ==Literature== * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|ja|ライナー・ハンニグ|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Great Hand Dictionary of Egyptian – German. (2800 – 950 BC). The Language of the Pharaohs'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = {{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史|12=draft|12=draft}}. Volume 64). Marburg Edition, 4th revised edition, by Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Dates. Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt'' (= ''Hildesheim Egyptological Contributions (HÄB)''. Volume 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Incorrect ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Memoirs of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class (AM-GS)''. 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz et al. 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} == Literature == * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|ja|ライナー・ハニヒ|zh|赖纳·汉尼格|12=draft}}: ''Großes Handwörterbuch Ägyptisch – Deutsch. (2800 – 950 v. Chr.). Die Sprache der Pharaonen'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = ''{{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史|zh|古代世界文化史|12=draft}}.'' Volume 64). Marburger Edition, 4th revised edition, von Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|zh|罗尔夫·克劳斯|12=draft}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer ägyptologische Beiträge. (HÄB).'' Volume 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Incorrect ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|12=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz et al., 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} ==References== {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories|[[Category:Religious Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Festival Calendar of the Month Achet II]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Festival calendar of month Achet II]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] }} [[sn:Chenep-scha]] [[de:Chenep-scha]] [[ja:Chenep-scha]] [[ja:砂を取る祭]] [[sn:Day of the Sand Holes]] [[en:Day of the Sand Holes]] [[de:Chenep-scha|12=draft]]

From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|セソストリスIIの葬祭寺|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリンパピルス10248|12=draft|12=draft}}) it is recorded in the 14th regnal year of Sesostris III that the announcement in the {{ill|Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|12=draft|12=draft}} for {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アチェット|12=draft|12=draft}} on the second lunar day was made. Since in the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|アクロバット団の祭日暦|12=draft|12=draft}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhet III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメネムヘトIII|12=draft|12=draft}} the Menchet Festival for the second month Achet was scheduled without a specified date, an announcement of the lunar date was not yet available at the time of the creation of that festival calendar. ==Literature== * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|ja|ライナー・ハンニグ|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Great Hand Dictionary of Egyptian – German. (2800 – 950 BC). The Language of the Pharaohs'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = {{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史|12=draft|12=draft}}. Volume 64). Marburg Edition, 4th revised edition, by Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Dates. Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt'' (= ''Hildesheim Egyptological Contributions (HÄB)''. Volume 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Incorrect ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Memoirs of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class (AM-GS)''. 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz et al. 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} == Literature == * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|ja|ライナー・ハニヒ|zh|赖纳·汉尼格|12=draft}}: ''Großes Handwörterbuch Ägyptisch – Deutsch. (2800 – 950 v. Chr.). Die Sprache der Pharaonen'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = ''{{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史|zh|古代世界文化史|12=draft}}.'' Volume 64). Marburger Edition, 4th revised edition, von Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|zh|罗尔夫·克劳斯|12=draft}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Incorrect ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|12=draft}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. '''{{PAGENAME}}''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Revise|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节|12=draft}}) The article currently has no inline references.}} {{Infobox Hieroglyphs Double Column |EXPANDED-NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国|12=draft}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Shifting Sand / Collecting Sand''
''Festival of Pouring Sand'' |EXPANDED-NAME2 = '''Festival date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION =''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second lunar day of the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Pyramid of Senusret II at Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also '''Day of Collecting Sand''') is documented from the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国|12=draft}} under the reign of King ([[Pharaoh]]) {{ill|Nyuserre Ini|de|Niuserre|ja|ニウセルラー|zh|纽塞拉|12=draft}}. It is associated with the birth of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯|12=draft}} and the goddess {{ill|Wosret|de|Wosret|ja|ウォスレト|zh|沃斯雷特|12=draft}}, attested in {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich|ja|中王国時代|zh|埃及中王国|12=draft}} at {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|de|Theben (Ägypten)|ja|テーベ (エジプト)|zh|底比斯|12=draft}}, who was revered as a protector of the youthful Horus. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the commonly observed first day of the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|zh|新月|12=draft}}, but involved the {{ill|ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|12=draft}} of ''filling the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Horusauge|ja|ホルスの目|zh|荷鲁斯之眼|12=draft}}'' on the second lunar day. The {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节|12=draft}} followed one day later on the third day, symbolizing the ''clothing of the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|zh|月亮|12=draft}}''. === 12th Dynasty === In the {{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|ja|エジプト第12王朝|zh|埃及第十二王朝|12=draft}}, under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|ja|センウセレト3世|zh|辛努塞尔特三世|12=draft}}, the festival was also named ''Day of Collecting Sand of Anubis on his mountain in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed''. The {{ill|Pyramid of Senusret II|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|ja|センウセレト2世のピラミッド|zh|辛努塞尔特二世金字塔|12=draft}} at {{ill|El Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|ja|エル・ラフーン|zh|拉洪|12=draft}} was traditionally assigned to the ''Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|zh|阿努比斯|12=draft}}''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary temple of Senusret II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|センウセレト2世葬祭殿|zh|辛努塞尔特二世的太庙|12=draft}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリン・パピルス10248|zh|柏林纸草文献10248|12=draft}}), the announcement is noted in the {{ill|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|古埃及阴历|12=draft}} for the 14th regnal year of Senusret III on the {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アケト|zh|阿赫特|12=draft}} 18, second lunar day. Since the {{ill|Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|曲芸団の祭典暦|zh|杂技团节日历|12=draft}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメンエムハト3世|zh|阿蒙涅姆赫特三世|12=draft}} scheduled the Menchet festival for the second month of Achet without specifying the date, a lunar date announcement had not been available at the time of creating the festival calendar. ==Overview 2== {{Improve||This article|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Discussion: Menchet-Fest|de|Diskussion: Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭の議論|12=draft|12=draft}}) The article does not yet contain any individual citation.}} {{Infobox Hieroglyphs Two-Column |EXTENDED NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国|12=draft|12=draft}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Sand Shifting / Sand Lifting''
''Festival of Sand Dispersion'' |EXTENDED NAME2 = '''Festival Date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION = ''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second day of the moon in the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Sesostris II Pyramid in Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also known as '''Day of Sand Lifting''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国|12=draft|12=draft}} under the reign of King ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|12=draft|12=draft}}) {{ill|Niuserre|de|Niuserre|ja|ニウセルレ|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is associated with the birth of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|12=draft|12=draft}} and the goddess {{ill|Wosret|de|Wosret|ja|ウォスレット|12=draft|12=draft}}, attested in the {{ill|Middle Kingdom|de|Mittleres Reich|ja|エジプト中王国|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|de|Theben (Ägypten)|ja|テーベ (エジプト)|12=draft|12=draft}}, who was venerated as the protector of the youthful Horus. ==Background== ===Old Kingdom=== The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the otherwise customary first day of the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|12=draft|12=draft}}, but instead included the {{ill|Ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式|12=draft|12=draft}} of the ''Filling of the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Horusauge|ja|ホルスの目|12=draft|12=draft}}'' on the second day of the lunar month. The {{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭|12=draft|12=draft}} followed one day later on the third day and symbolized the ''Dressing of the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|12=draft|12=draft}}''. ===12th Dynasty=== In the {{ill|12th Dynasty|de|12. Dynastie|ja|第12王朝|12=draft|12=draft}}, under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.|ja|セソストリスIII|12=draft|12=draft}}, the designation ''Day of the Sand Lifting of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|12=draft|12=draft}} on his mountain in the pyramid city of Sesostris II, the Blessed'' was also used. The {{ill|Sesostris II Pyramid|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|ja|セソストリスIIのピラミッド|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Al-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|ja|アル・ラフーン|12=draft|12=draft}} was traditionally assigned, by virtue of its location, to the ''Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|12=draft|12=draft}}''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|セソストリスIIの葬祭寺|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリンパピルス10248|12=draft|12=draft}}) it is recorded in the 14th regnal year of Sesostris III that the announcement in the {{ill|Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|12=draft|12=draft}} for {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アチェット|12=draft|12=draft}} on the second lunar day was made. Since in the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|アクロバット団の祭日暦|12=draft|12=draft}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhet III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメネムヘトIII|12=draft|12=draft}} the Menchet Festival for the second month Achet was scheduled without a specified date, an announcement of the lunar date was not yet available at the time of the creation of that festival calendar. ==Literature== * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|ja|ライナー・ハンニグ|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Great Hand Dictionary of Egyptian – German. (2800 – 950 BC). The Language of the Pharaohs'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = {{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史|12=draft|12=draft}}. Volume 64). Marburg Edition, 4th revised edition, by Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Dates. Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt'' (= ''Hildesheim Egyptological Contributions (HÄB)''. Volume 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Incorrect ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|セソストリスIIの葬祭寺|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリンパピルス10248|12=draft|12=draft}}) it is recorded in the 14th regnal year of Sesostris III that the announcement in the {{ill|Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|12=draft|12=draft}} for {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アチェット|12=draft|12=draft}} on the second lunar day was made. Since in the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|アクロバット団の祭日暦|12=draft|12=draft}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhet III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメネムヘトIII|12=draft|12=draft}} the Menchet Festival for the second month Achet was scheduled without a specified date, an announcement of the lunar date was not yet available at the time of the creation of that festival calendar. ==Literature== * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|ja|ライナー・ハンニグ|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Great Hand Dictionary of Egyptian – German. '''Day of the Sand Holes''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Revise|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节|12=draft}}) The article currently has no inline references.}} {{Infobox Hieroglyphs Double Column |EXPANDED-NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国|12=draft}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Shifting Sand / Collecting Sand''
''Festival of Pouring Sand'' |EXPANDED-NAME2 = '''Festival date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION =''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second lunar day of the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Pyramid of Senusret II at Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also '''Day of Collecting Sand''') is documented from the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国|12=draft}} under the reign of King ([[Pharaoh]]) {{ill|Nyuserre Ini|de|Niuserre|ja|ニウセルラー|zh|纽塞拉|12=draft}}. It is associated with the birth of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯|12=draft}} and the goddess {{ill|Wosret|de|Wosret|ja|ウォスレト|zh|沃斯雷特|12=draft}}, attested in {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich|ja|中王国時代|zh|埃及中王国|12=draft}} at {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|de|Theben (Ägypten)|ja|テーベ (エジプト)|zh|底比斯|12=draft}}, who was revered as a protector of the youthful Horus. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the commonly observed first day of the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|zh|新月|12=draft}}, but involved the {{ill|ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|12=draft}} of ''filling the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Horusauge|ja|ホルスの目|zh|荷鲁斯之眼|12=draft}}'' on the second lunar day. The {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节|12=draft}} followed one day later on the third day, symbolizing the ''clothing of the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|zh|月亮|12=draft}}''. === 12th Dynasty === In the {{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|ja|エジプト第12王朝|zh|埃及第十二王朝|12=draft}}, under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|ja|センウセレト3世|zh|辛努塞尔特三世|12=draft}}, the festival was also named ''Day of Collecting Sand of Anubis on his mountain in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed''. The {{ill|Pyramid of Senusret II|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|ja|センウセレト2世のピラミッド|zh|辛努塞尔特二世金字塔|12=draft}} at {{ill|El Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|ja|エル・ラフーン|zh|拉洪|12=draft}} was traditionally assigned to the ''Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|zh|阿努比斯|12=draft}}''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary temple of Senusret II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|センウセレト2世葬祭殿|zh|辛努塞尔特二世的太庙|12=draft}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリン・パピルス10248|zh|柏林纸草文献10248|12=draft}}), the announcement is noted in the {{ill|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|古埃及阴历|12=draft}} for the 14th regnal year of Senusret III on the {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アケト|zh|阿赫特|12=draft}} 18, second lunar day. Since the {{ill|Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|曲芸団の祭典暦|zh|杂技团节日历|12=draft}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメンエムハト3世|zh|阿蒙涅姆赫特三世|12=draft}} scheduled the Menchet festival for the second month of Achet without specifying the date, a lunar date announcement had not been available at the time of creating the festival calendar. ==Overview 2== {{Improve||This article|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Discussion: Menchet-Fest|de|Diskussion: Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭の議論|12=draft|12=draft}}) The article does not yet contain any individual citation.}} {{Infobox Hieroglyphs Two-Column |EXTENDED NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国|12=draft|12=draft}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Sand Shifting / Sand Lifting''
''Festival of Sand Dispersion'' |EXTENDED NAME2 = '''Festival Date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION = ''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second day of the moon in the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Sesostris II Pyramid in Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also known as '''Day of Sand Lifting''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国|12=draft|12=draft}} under the reign of King ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|12=draft|12=draft}}) {{ill|Niuserre|de|Niuserre|ja|ニウセルレ|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is associated with the birth of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|12=draft|12=draft}} and the goddess {{ill|Wosret|de|Wosret|ja|ウォスレット|12=draft|12=draft}}, attested in the {{ill|Middle Kingdom|de|Mittleres Reich|ja|エジプト中王国|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|de|Theben (Ägypten)|ja|テーベ (エジプト)|12=draft|12=draft}}, who was venerated as the protector of the youthful Horus. ==Background== ===Old Kingdom=== The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the otherwise customary first day of the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|12=draft|12=draft}}, but instead included the {{ill|Ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式|12=draft|12=draft}} of the ''Filling of the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Horusauge|ja|ホルスの目|12=draft|12=draft}}'' on the second day of the lunar month. The {{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭|12=draft|12=draft}} followed one day later on the third day and symbolized the ''Dressing of the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|12=draft|12=draft}}''. ===12th Dynasty=== In the {{ill|12th Dynasty|de|12. Dynastie|ja|第12王朝|12=draft|12=draft}}, under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.|ja|セソストリスIII|12=draft|12=draft}}, the designation ''Day of the Sand Lifting of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|12=draft|12=draft}} on his mountain in the pyramid city of Sesostris II, the Blessed'' was also used. The {{ill|Sesostris II Pyramid|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|ja|セソストリスIIのピラミッド|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Al-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|ja|アル・ラフーン|12=draft|12=draft}} was traditionally assigned, by virtue of its location, to the ''Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|12=draft|12=draft}}''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|セソストリスIIの葬祭寺|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリンパピルス10248|12=draft|12=draft}}) it is recorded in the 14th regnal year of Sesostris III that the announcement in the {{ill|Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|12=draft|12=draft}} for {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アチェット|12=draft|12=draft}} on the second lunar day was made. Since in the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|アクロバット団の祭日暦|12=draft|12=draft}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhet III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメネムヘトIII|12=draft|12=draft}} the Menchet Festival for the second month Achet was scheduled without a specified date, an announcement of the lunar date was not yet available at the time of the creation of that festival calendar. ==Literature== * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|ja|ライナー・ハンニグ|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Great Hand Dictionary of Egyptian – German. Dynastie|ja|第12王朝|12=draft|12=draft}}, under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.|ja|セソストリスIII|12=draft|12=draft}}, the designation ''Day of the Sand Lifting of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|12=draft|12=draft}} on his mountain in the pyramid city of Sesostris II, the Blessed'' was also used. The {{ill|Sesostris II Pyramid|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|ja|セソストリスIIのピラミッド|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Al-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|ja|アル・ラフーン|12=draft|12=draft}} was traditionally assigned, by virtue of its location, to the ''Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|12=draft|12=draft}}''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|セソストリスIIの葬祭寺|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリンパピルス10248|12=draft|12=draft}}) it is recorded in the 14th regnal year of Sesostris III that the announcement in the {{ill|Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|12=draft|12=draft}} for {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アチェット|12=draft|12=draft}} on the second lunar day was made. Since in the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|アクロバット団の祭日暦|12=draft|12=draft}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhet III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメネムヘトIII|12=draft|12=draft}} the Menchet Festival for the second month Achet was scheduled without a specified date, an announcement of the lunar date was not yet available at the time of the creation of that festival calendar. ==Literature== * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|ja|ライナー・ハンニグ|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Great Hand Dictionary of Egyptian – German. (2800 – 950 BC). The Language of the Pharaohs'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = {{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史|12=draft|12=draft}}. Volume 64). Marburg Edition, 4th revised edition, by Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Dates. Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt'' (= ''Hildesheim Egyptological Contributions (HÄB)''. Volume 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Incorrect ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Memoirs of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class (AM-GS)''. 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz et al. 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar}} == Literature == * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|ja|ライナー・ハニヒ|zh|赖纳·汉尼格|12=draft}}: ''Großes Handwörterbuch Ägyptisch – Deutsch. (2800 – 950 v. Chr.). Die Sprache der Pharaonen'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = ''{{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史|zh|古代世界文化史|12=draft}}.'' Volume 64).
