'''Day of the Sand Holes''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Revise|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet-Festival|qq=}}) The article currently has no inline references.|qq=}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |EXPANDED-NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom of Egypt|qq=}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Shifting Sand / Collecting Sand''
''Festival of Pouring Sand'' |EXPANDED-NAME2 = '''Festival date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION =''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second lunar day of the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Pyramid of Senusret II at Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also '''Day of Collecting Sand''') is documented from the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom of Egypt|qq=}} under the reign of King ([[Pharaoh]]) {{ill|Nyuserre Ini|de|Niuserre|ja|ニウセルラー|zh|纽塞拉|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyuserre Ini|qq=}}. It is associated with the birth of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|zh|荷鲁斯|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horus|qq=}} and the goddess {{ill|Wosret|de|Wosret|ja|ウォスレト|zh|沃斯雷特|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wosret|qq=}}, attested in {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich|ja|中王国時代|zh|埃及中王国|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kingdom of Egypt|qq=}} at {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|de|Theben (Ägypten)|ja|テーベ (エジプト)|zh|底比斯|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thebes, Egypt|qq=}}, who was revered as a protector of the youthful Horus. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the commonly observed first day of the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|zh|新月|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Moon|qq=}}, but involved the {{ill|ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ritual|qq=}} of ''filling the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Horusauge|ja|ホルスの目|zh|荷鲁斯之眼|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eye of Horus|qq=}}'' on the second lunar day. The {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet-Festival|qq=}} followed one day later on the third day, symbolizing the ''clothing of the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|zh|月亮|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moon|qq=}}''. === 12th Dynasty === In the {{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|ja|エジプト第12王朝|zh|埃及第十二王朝|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|qq=}}, under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|ja|センウセレト3世|zh|辛努塞尔特三世|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret III|qq=}}, the festival was also named ''Day of Collecting Sand of Anubis on his mountain in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed''. The {{ill|Pyramid of Senusret II|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|ja|センウセレト2世のピラミッド|zh|辛努塞尔特二世金字塔|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pyramid of Senusret II|qq=}} at {{ill|El Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|ja|エル・ラフーン|zh|拉洪|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/El Lahun|qq=}} was traditionally assigned to the ''Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|zh|阿努比斯|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anubis|qq=}}''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary temple of Senusret II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|センウセレト2世葬祭殿|zh|辛努塞尔特二世的太庙|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mortuary temple of Senusret II|qq=}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリン・パピルス10248|zh|柏林纸草文献10248|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Berlin 10248|qq=}}), the announcement is noted in the {{ill|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|古埃及阴历|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}} for the 14th regnal year of Senusret III on the {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アケト|zh|阿赫特|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet|qq=}} 18, second lunar day. Since the {{ill|Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|曲芸団の祭典暦|zh|杂技团节日历|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|qq=}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhat III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメンエムハト3世|zh|阿蒙涅姆赫特三世|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenemhat III|qq=}} scheduled the Menchet festival for the second month of Achet without specifying the date, a lunar date announcement had not been available at the time of creating the festival calendar. ==Overview 2== {{Improve||This article|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Discussion: Menchet-Fest|de|Diskussion: Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭の議論|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Discussion: Menchet-Fest|qq=}}) The article does not yet contain any individual citation.|qq=}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs Two-Column |EXTENDED NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Sand Shifting / Sand Lifting''
''Festival of Sand Dispersion'' |EXTENDED NAME2 = '''Festival Date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION = ''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second day of the moon in the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Sesostris II Pyramid in Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also known as '''Day of Sand Lifting''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=}} under the reign of King ({{ill|Pharaoh|de|Pharao|ja|ファラオ|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pharaoh|qq=}}) {{ill|Niuserre|de|Niuserre|ja|ニウセルレ|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niuserre|qq=}}. It is associated with the birth of {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|ja|ホルス|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horus|qq=}} and the goddess {{ill|Wosret|de|Wosret|ja|ウォスレット|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wosret|qq=}}, attested in the {{ill|Middle Kingdom|de|Mittleres Reich|ja|エジプト中王国|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kingdom|qq=}} at {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|de|Theben (Ägypten)|ja|テーベ (エジプト)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thebes (Egypt)|qq=}}, who was venerated as the protector of the youthful Horus. ==Background== ===Old Kingdom=== The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the otherwise customary first day of the {{ill|New Moon|de|Neumond|ja|新月|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Moon|qq=}}, but instead included the {{ill|Ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritual|qq=}} of the ''Filling of the {{ill|Eye of Horus|de|Horusauge|ja|ホルスの目|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eye of Horus|qq=}}'' on the second day of the lunar month. The {{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet Festival|qq=}} followed one day later on the third day and symbolized the ''Dressing of the {{ill|Moon|de|Mond|ja|月|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moon|qq=}}''. ===12th Dynasty=== In the {{ill|12th Dynasty|de|12. Dynastie|ja|第12王朝|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/12th Dynasty|qq=}}, under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III.|ja|セソストリスIII|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sesostris III|qq=}}, the designation ''Day of the Sand Lifting of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anubis|qq=}} on his mountain in the pyramid city of Sesostris II, the Blessed'' was also used. The {{ill|Sesostris II Pyramid|de|Sesostris-II.-Pyramide|ja|セソストリスIIのピラミッド|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sesostris II Pyramid|qq=}} of {{ill|Al-Lahun|de|Al-Lahun|ja|アル・ラフーン|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Al-Lahun|qq=}} was traditionally assigned, by virtue of its location, to the ''Mountain of {{ill|Anubis|de|Anubis|ja|アヌビス|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anubis|qq=}}''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|セソストリスIIの葬祭寺|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|qq=}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリンパピルス10248|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Berlin 10248|qq=}}) it is recorded in the 14th regnal year of Sesostris III that the announcement in the {{ill|Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=}} for {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|ja|アチェット|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet|qq=}} on the second lunar day was made. Since in the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|アクロバット団の祭日暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|qq=}} from the 35th regnal year of {{ill|Amenemhet III|de|Amenemhet III.|ja|アメネムヘトIII|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenemhet III|qq=}} the Menchet Festival for the second month Achet was scheduled without a specified date, an announcement of the lunar date was not yet available at the time of the creation of that festival calendar. ==Literature== * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|ja|ライナー・ハンニグ|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rainer Hannig|qq=}}: ''Great Hand Dictionary of Egyptian – German. (2800 – 950 BC). The Language of the Pharaohs'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = {{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural History of the Ancient World|qq=}}. Volume 64). Marburg Edition, 4th revised edition, by Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Dates. Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt'' (= ''Hildesheim Egyptological Contributions (HÄB)''. Volume 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Incorrect ISBN|3-8067-8086-X|qq=}}. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Memoirs of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class (AM-GS)''. 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977|qq=}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz et al. 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar|qq=}} == Literature == * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|ja|ライナー・ハニヒ|zh|赖纳·汉尼格|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rainer Hannig|qq=}}: ''Großes Handwörterbuch Ägyptisch – Deutsch. (2800 – 950 v. Chr.). Die Sprache der Pharaonen'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = ''{{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史|zh|古代世界文化史|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural History of the Ancient World|qq=}}.'' Volume 64). Marburger Edition, 4th revised edition, von Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|zh|罗尔夫·克劳斯|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer ägyptologische Beiträge. (HÄB).'' Volume 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Incorrect ISBN|3-8067-8086-X|qq=}}. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977|qq=}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz et al., 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar|qq=}} ==References== {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar|qq=}} {{translated page|de|Chenep-scha|version=217297418|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Festival Calendar of the Month Achet II]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Festival calendar of month Achet II]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Day of the Sand Holes]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Day of the Sand Holes]][[Category:qq]] [[sn: failed]] }} [[sn: failed]] [[sn:Chenep-scha]] [[de:Chenep-scha]] [[sn:henep-scha]] [[ja:砂を取る祭]] [[sn:Day of the Sand Holes]] [[en:Day of the Sand Holes]]

revision.text:
'''Day of the Sand Holes''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Revise|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet-Festival|qq=}}) The article currently has no inline references.|qq=}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |EXPANDED-NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom of Egypt|qq=}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Shifting Sand / Collecting Sand''
''Festival of Pouring Sand'' |EXPANDED-NAME2 = '''Festival date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION =''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second lunar day of the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Pyramid of Senusret II at Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also '''Day of Collecting Sand''') is documented from the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom of Egypt|qq=}} under the reign of King ([[Pharaoh]]) [[:en:Nyuserre Ini|Nyuserre Ini]]. It is associated with the birth of [[:en:Horus|Horus]] and the goddess [[:en:Wosret|Wosret]], attested in [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom of Egypt]] at [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes, Egypt]], who was revered as a protector of the youthful Horus. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the commonly observed first day of the [[:en:New moon|New Moon]], but involved the {{ill|ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ritual|qq=}} of ''filling the [[:en:Eye of Horus|Eye of Horus]]'' on the second lunar day. The {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet-Festival|qq=}} followed one day later on the third day, symbolizing the ''clothing of the [[:en:Moon|Moon]]''. === 12th Dynasty === In the [[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]], under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]], the festival was also named ''Day of Collecting Sand of Anubis on his mountain in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed''. The [[:en:Pyramid of Senusret II|Pyramid of Senusret II]] at [[:en:El Lahun|El Lahun]] was traditionally assigned to the ''Mountain of [[:en:Anubis|Anubis]]''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary temple of Senusret II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|センウセレト2世葬祭殿|zh|辛努塞尔特二世的太庙|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mortuary temple of Senusret II|qq=}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリン・パピルス10248|zh|柏林纸草文献10248|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Berlin 10248|qq=}}), the announcement is noted in the {{ill|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|古埃及阴历|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}} for the 14th regnal year of Senusret III on the [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Achet]] 18, second lunar day. Since the {{ill|Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|曲芸団の祭典暦|zh|杂技团节日历|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|qq=}} from the 35th regnal year of [[:en:Amenemhat III|Amenemhat III]] scheduled the Menchet festival for the second month of Achet without specifying the date, a lunar date announcement had not been available at the time of creating the festival calendar. ==Overview 2== {{Improve||This article|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Discussion: Menchet-Fest|de|Diskussion: Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭の議論|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Discussion: Menchet-Fest|qq=}}) The article does not yet contain any individual citation.|qq=}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs Two-Column |EXTENDED NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Sand Shifting / Sand Lifting''
''Festival of Sand Dispersion'' |EXTENDED NAME2 = '''Festival Date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION = ''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second day of the moon in the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Sesostris II Pyramid in Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also known as '''Day of Sand Lifting''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=}} under the reign of King ([[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]]) [[:en:Nyuserre Ini|Niuserre]]. It is associated with the birth of [[:en:Horus|Horus]] and the goddess [[:en:Wosret|Wosret]], attested in the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom]] at [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes (Egypt)]], who was venerated as the protector of the youthful Horus. ==Background== ===Old Kingdom=== The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the otherwise customary first day of the [[:en:New moon|New Moon]], but instead included the {{ill|Ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritual|qq=}} of the ''Filling of the [[:en:Eye of Horus|Eye of Horus]]'' on the second day of the lunar month. The {{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet Festival|qq=}} followed one day later on the third day and symbolized the ''Dressing of the [[:en:Moon|Moon]]''. ===12th Dynasty=== In the [[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|12th Dynasty]], under [[:en:Senusret III|Sesostris III]], the designation ''Day of the Sand Lifting of [[:en:Anubis|Anubis]] on his mountain in the pyramid city of Sesostris II, the Blessed'' was also used. The [[:en:Pyramid of Senusret II|Sesostris II Pyramid]] of [[:en:El Lahun|Al-Lahun]] was traditionally assigned, by virtue of its location, to the ''Mountain of [[:en:Anubis|Anubis]]''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|セソストリスIIの葬祭寺|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|qq=}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリンパピルス10248|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Berlin 10248|qq=}}) it is recorded in the 14th regnal year of Sesostris III that the announcement in the {{ill|Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=}} for [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Achet]] on the second lunar day was made. Since in the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|アクロバット団の祭日暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|qq=}} from the 35th regnal year of [[:en:Amenemhat III|Amenemhet III]] the Menchet Festival for the second month Achet was scheduled without a specified date, an announcement of the lunar date was not yet available at the time of the creation of that festival calendar. ==Literature== * [[:en:Rainer Hannig|Rainer Hannig]]: ''Great Hand Dictionary of Egyptian – German. (2800 – 950 BC). The Language of the Pharaohs'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = {{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural History of the Ancient World|qq=}}. Volume 64). revision.text:
'''Day of the Sand Holes''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Revise|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet-Festival|qq=}}) The article currently has no inline references.|qq=}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |EXPANDED-NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom of Egypt|qq=}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Shifting Sand / Collecting Sand''
''Festival of Pouring Sand'' |EXPANDED-NAME2 = '''Festival date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION =''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second lunar day of the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Pyramid of Senusret II at Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also '''Day of Collecting Sand''') is documented from the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom of Egypt|qq=}} under the reign of King ([[Pharaoh]]) [[:en:Nyuserre Ini|Nyuserre Ini]]. It is associated with the birth of [[:en:Horus|Horus]] and the goddess [[:en:Wosret|Wosret]], attested in [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom of Egypt]] at [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes, Egypt]], who was revered as a protector of the youthful Horus. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the commonly observed first day of the [[:en:New moon|New Moon]], but involved the {{ill|ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式|zh|仪式|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ritual|qq=}} of ''filling the [[:en:Eye of Horus|Eye of Horus]]'' on the second lunar day. The {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet-Festival|qq=}} followed one day later on the third day, symbolizing the ''clothing of the [[:en:Moon|Moon]]''. === 12th Dynasty === In the [[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]], under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]], the festival was also named ''Day of Collecting Sand of Anubis on his mountain in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed''. The [[:en:Pyramid of Senusret II|Pyramid of Senusret II]] at [[:en:El Lahun|El Lahun]] was traditionally assigned to the ''Mountain of [[:en:Anubis|Anubis]]''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary temple of Senusret II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|センウセレト2世葬祭殿|zh|辛努塞尔特二世的太庙|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mortuary temple of Senusret II|qq=}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリン・パピルス10248|zh|柏林纸草文献10248|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Berlin 10248|qq=}}), the announcement is noted in the {{ill|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|古埃及阴历|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}} for the 14th regnal year of Senusret III on the [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Achet]] 18, second lunar day. Since the {{ill|Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|曲芸団の祭典暦|zh|杂技团节日历|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|qq=}} from the 35th regnal year of [[:en:Amenemhat III|Amenemhat III]] scheduled the Menchet festival for the second month of Achet without specifying the date, a lunar date announcement had not been available at the time of creating the festival calendar. ==Overview 2== {{Improve||This article|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Discussion: Menchet-Fest|de|Diskussion: Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭の議論|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Discussion: Menchet-Fest|qq=}}) The article does not yet contain any individual citation.|qq=}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |EXTENDED NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Sand Shifting / Sand Lifting''
''Festival of Sand Dispersion'' |EXTENDED NAME2 = '''Festival Date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION = ''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second day of the moon in the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Sesostris II Pyramid in Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also known as '''Day of Sand Lifting''') has been attested since the {{ill|Old Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|エジプト古王国|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom (Egypt)|qq=}} under the reign of King ([[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]]) [[:en:Nyuserre Ini|Niuserre]]. It is associated with the birth of [[:en:Horus|Horus]] and the goddess [[:en:Wosret|Wosret]], attested in the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom]] at [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes (Egypt)]], who was venerated as the protector of the youthful Horus. ==Background== ===Old Kingdom=== The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the otherwise customary first day of the [[:en:New moon|New Moon]], but instead included the {{ill|Ritual|de|Ritus|ja|儀式|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritual|qq=}} of the ''Filling of the [[:en:Eye of Horus|Eye of Horus]]'' on the second day of the lunar month. The {{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet Festival|qq=}} followed one day later on the third day and symbolized the ''Dressing of the [[:en:Moon|Moon]]''. ===12th Dynasty=== In the [[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|12th Dynasty]], under [[:en:Senusret III|Sesostris III]], the designation ''Day of the Sand Lifting of [[:en:Anubis|Anubis]] on his mountain in the pyramid city of Sesostris II, the Blessed'' was also used. The [[:en:Pyramid of Senusret II|Sesostris II Pyramid]] of [[:en:El Lahun|Al-Lahun]] was traditionally assigned, by virtue of its location, to the ''Mountain of [[:en:Anubis|Anubis]]''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|セソストリスIIの葬祭寺|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|qq=}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリンパピルス10248|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Berlin 10248|qq=}}) it is recorded in the 14th regnal year of Sesostris III that the announcement in the {{ill|Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=}} for [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Achet]] on the second lunar day was made. Since in the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|アクロバット団の祭日暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|qq=}} from the 35th regnal year of [[:en:Amenemhat III|Amenemhet III]] the Menchet Festival for the second month Achet was scheduled without a specified date, an announcement of the lunar date was not yet available at the time of the creation of that festival calendar. ==Literature== * [[:en:Rainer Hannig|Rainer Hannig]]: ''Great Hand Dictionary of Egyptian – German. (2800 – 950 BC). The Language of the Pharaohs'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = {{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural History of the Ancient World|qq=}}. Volume 64). Marburg Edition, 4th revised edition, by Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Dates. Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt'' (= ''Hildesheim Egyptological Contributions (HÄB)''. Volume 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Incorrect ISBN|3-8067-8086-X|qq=}}. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Memoirs of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class (AM-GS)''. 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977|qq=}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz et al. 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar|qq=}} == Literature == * [[:en:Rainer Hannig|Rainer Hannig]]: ''Großes Handwörterbuch Ägyptisch – Deutsch. (2800 – 950 v. Chr.). Die Sprache der Pharaonen'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = ''{{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史|zh|古代世界文化史|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural History of the Ancient World|qq=}}.'' Volume 64). Marburger Edition, 4th revised edition, von Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|zh|罗尔夫·克劳斯|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer ägyptologische Beiträge. (HÄB).'' Volume 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Incorrect ISBN|3-8067-8086-X|qq=}}. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977|qq=}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur et al., Mainz et al., 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar|qq=}} ==References== {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar|qq=}} {{translated page|de|Chenep-scha|version=217297418|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Festival Calendar of the Month Achet II]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Religious festivals (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Festival calendar of month Achet II]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Day of the Sand Holes]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Day of the Sand Holes]][[Category:qq]] [[sn: failed]] }} [[sn: failed]] [[sn:Chenep-scha]] [[de:Chenep-scha]] [[sn:henep-scha]] [[ja:砂を取る祭]] [[sn:Day of the Sand Holes]] [[en:Day of the Sand Holes]]

The [[:en:Pyramid of Senusret II|Sesostris II Pyramid]] of [[:en:El Lahun|Al-Lahun]] was traditionally assigned, by virtue of its location, to the ''Mountain of [[:en:Anubis|Anubis]]''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|セソストリスIIの葬祭寺|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|qq=}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリンパピルス10248|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Berlin 10248|qq=}}) it is recorded in the 14th regnal year of Sesostris III that the announcement in the {{ill|Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=}} for [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Achet]] on the second lunar day was made. Since in the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|lt=Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|lt=Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|アクロバット団の祭日暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|qq=}} from the 35th regnal year of [[:en:Amenemhat III|Amenemhet III]] the Menchet Festival for the second month Achet was scheduled without a specified date, an announcement of the lunar date was not yet available at the time of the creation of that festival calendar. ==Literature== * [[:en:Rainer Hannig|Rainer Hannig]]: ''Great Hand Dictionary of Egyptian – German. (2800 – 950 BC). The Language of the Pharaohs'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = {{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural History of the Ancient World|qq=}}. Volume 64). Marburg Edition, 4th revised edition, by Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Dates. revision.text:
'''Day of the Sand Holes''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Revise|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet-Festival|qq=}}) The article currently has no inline references.|qq=}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |EXPANDED-NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom of Egypt|qq=}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Shifting Sand / Collecting Sand''
''Festival of Pouring Sand'' |EXPANDED-NAME2 = '''Festival date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION =''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second lunar day of the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Pyramid of Senusret II at Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also '''Day of Collecting Sand''') is documented from the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom of Egypt|qq=}} under the reign of King ([[Pharaoh]]) [[:en:Nyuserre Ini|Nyuserre Ini]]. It is associated with the birth of [[:en:Horus|Horus]] and the goddess [[:en:Wosret|Wosret]], attested in [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom of Egypt]] at [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes, Egypt]], who was revered as a protector of the youthful Horus. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the commonly observed first day of the [[:en:New moon|New Moon]], but involved the [[:en:Ritual|ritual]] of ''filling the [[:en:Eye of Horus|Eye of Horus]]'' on the second lunar day. The {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet-Festival|qq=}} followed one day later on the third day, symbolizing the ''clothing of the [[:en:Moon|Moon]]''. === 12th Dynasty === In the [[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]], under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]], the festival was also named ''Day of Collecting Sand of Anubis on his mountain in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed''. The [[:en:Pyramid of Senusret II|Pyramid of Senusret II]] at [[:en:El Lahun|El Lahun]] was traditionally assigned to the ''Mountain of [[:en:Anubis|Anubis]]''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary temple of Senusret II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|センウセレト2世葬祭殿|zh|辛努塞尔特二世的太庙|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mortuary temple of Senusret II|qq=}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリン・パピルス10248|zh|柏林纸草文献10248|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Berlin 10248|qq=}}), the announcement is noted in the {{ill|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|古埃及阴历|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}} for the 14th regnal year of Senusret III on the [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Achet]] 18, second lunar day. Since the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|lt=Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|lt=Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|曲芸団の祭典暦|zh|杂技团节日历|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|qq=}} from the 35th regnal year of [[:en:Amenemhat III|Amenemhat III]] scheduled the Menchet festival for the second month of Achet without specifying the date, a lunar date announcement had not been available at the time of creating the festival calendar. ==Overview 2== {{Improve||This article|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Discussion: Menchet-Fest|de|Diskussion: Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭の議論|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Discussion: Menchet-Fest|qq=}}) The article does not yet contain any individual citation.|qq=}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |EXTENDED NAME = [[:en:Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom (Egypt)]] |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Sand Shifting / Sand Lifting''
''Festival of Sand Dispersion'' |EXTENDED NAME2 = '''Festival Date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION = ''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second day of the moon in the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Sesostris II Pyramid in Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also known as '''Day of Sand Lifting''') has been attested since the [[:en:Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom (Egypt)]] under the reign of King ([[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]]) [[:en:Nyuserre Ini|Niuserre]]. It is associated with the birth of [[:en:Horus|Horus]] and the goddess [[:en:Wosret|Wosret]], attested in the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom]] at [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes (Egypt)]], who was venerated as the protector of the youthful Horus. ==Background== ===Old Kingdom=== The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the otherwise customary first day of the [[:en:New moon|New Moon]], but instead included the [[:en:Ritual|Ritual]] of the ''Filling of the [[:en:Eye of Horus|Eye of Horus]]'' on the second day of the lunar month. The {{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet Festival|qq=}} followed one day later on the third day and symbolized the ''Dressing of the [[:en:Moon|Moon]]''. ===12th Dynasty=== In the [[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|12th Dynasty]], under [[:en:Senusret III|Sesostris III]], the designation ''Day of the Sand Lifting of [[:en:Anubis|Anubis]] on his mountain in the pyramid city of Sesostris II, the Blessed'' was also used. The [[:en:Pyramid of Senusret II|Sesostris II Pyramid]] of [[:en:El Lahun|Al-Lahun]] was traditionally assigned, by virtue of its location, to the ''Mountain of [[:en:Anubis|Anubis]]''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|セソストリスIIの葬祭寺|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|qq=}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリンパピルス10248|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Berlin 10248|qq=}}) it is recorded in the 14th regnal year of Sesostris III that the announcement in the {{ill|Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=}} for [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Achet]] on the second lunar day was made. Since in the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|lt=Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|lt=Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|アクロバット団の祭日暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|qq=}} from the 35th regnal year of [[:en:Amenemhat III|Amenemhet III]] the Menchet Festival for the second month Achet was scheduled without a specified date, an announcement of the lunar date was not yet available at the time of the creation of that festival calendar. ==Literature== * [[:en:Rainer Hannig|Rainer Hannig]]: ''Great Hand Dictionary of Egyptian – German. (2800 – 950 BC). The Language of the Pharaohs'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = {{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural History of the Ancient World|qq=}}. Volume 64). Marburg Edition, 4th revised edition, by Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Dates. Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt'' (= ''Hildesheim Egyptological Contributions (HÄB)''. Volume 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Incorrect ISBN|3-8067-8086-X|qq=}}. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Memoirs of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class (AM-GS)''. 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977|qq=}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz et al. 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar|qq=}} == Literature == * [[:en:Rainer Hannig|Rainer Hannig]]: ''Großes Handwörterbuch Ägyptisch – Deutsch. (2800 – 950 v. Chr.). Die Sprache der Pharaonen'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 64). Marburger Edition, 4th revised edition, von Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|zh|罗尔夫·克劳斯|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary temple of Senusret II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|センウセレト2世葬祭殿|zh|辛努塞尔特二世的太庙|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mortuary temple of Senusret II|qq=}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリン・パピルス10248|zh|柏林纸草文献10248|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Berlin 10248|qq=}}), the announcement is noted in the {{ill|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|古埃及阴历|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}} for the 14th regnal year of Senusret III on the [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Achet]] 18, second lunar day. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Discussion: Menchet-Fest|de|Diskussion: Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭の議論|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Discussion: Menchet-Fest|qq=}}) The article does not yet contain any individual citation.|qq=}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |EXTENDED NAME = [[:en:Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom (Egypt)]] |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Sand Shifting / Sand Lifting''
''Festival of Sand Dispersion'' |EXTENDED NAME2 = '''Festival Date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION = ''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second day of the moon in the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Sesostris II Pyramid in Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also known as '''Day of Sand Lifting''') has been attested since the [[:en:Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom (Egypt)]] under the reign of King ([[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]]) [[:en:Nyuserre Ini|Niuserre]]. It is associated with the birth of [[:en:Horus|Horus]] and the goddess [[:en:Wosret|Wosret]], attested in the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom]] at [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes (Egypt)]], who was venerated as the protector of the youthful Horus. ==Background== ===Old Kingdom=== The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the otherwise customary first day of the [[:en:New moon|New Moon]], but instead included the [[:en:Ritual|Ritual]] of the ''Filling of the [[:en:Eye of Horus|Eye of Horus]]'' on the second day of the lunar month. The {{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet Festival|qq=}} followed one day later on the third day and symbolized the ''Dressing of the [[:en:Moon|Moon]]''. ===12th Dynasty=== In the [[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|12th Dynasty]], under [[:en:Senusret III|Sesostris III]], the designation ''Day of the Sand Lifting of [[:en:Anubis|Anubis]] on his mountain in the pyramid city of Sesostris II, the Blessed'' was also used. The [[:en:Pyramid of Senusret II|Sesostris II Pyramid]] of [[:en:El Lahun|Al-Lahun]] was traditionally assigned, by virtue of its location, to the ''Mountain of [[:en:Anubis|Anubis]]''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|セソストリスIIの葬祭寺|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|qq=}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリンパピルス10248|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Berlin 10248|qq=}}) it is recorded in the 14th regnal year of Sesostris III that the announcement in the {{ill|Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=}} for [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Achet]] on the second lunar day was made. Since in the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|lt=Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|lt=Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|アクロバット団の祭日暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|qq=}} from the 35th regnal year of [[:en:Amenemhat III|Amenemhet III]] the Menchet Festival for the second month Achet was scheduled without a specified date, an announcement of the lunar date was not yet available at the time of the creation of that festival calendar. ==Literature== * [[:en:Rainer Hannig|Rainer Hannig]]: ''Great Hand Dictionary of Egyptian – German. (2800 – 950 BC). The Language of the Pharaohs'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = {{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural History of the Ancient World|qq=}}. Volume 64). Marburg Edition, 4th revised edition, by Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Dates. Studies on the Astronomical and Technical Chronology of Ancient Egypt'' (= ''Hildesheim Egyptological Contributions (HÄB)''. Volume 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Incorrect ISBN|3-8067-8086-X|qq=}}. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Memoirs of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class (AM-GS)''. 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977|qq=}}). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz et al. 1950. {{Navigation bar Egyptian Calendar|qq=}} == Literature == * [[:en:Rainer Hannig|Rainer Hannig]]: ''Großes Handwörterbuch Ägyptisch – Deutsch. (2800 – 950 v. Chr.). Die Sprache der Pharaonen'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = ''{{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史|zh|古代世界文化史|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural History of the Ancient World|qq=}}.'' Volume 64). Marburger Edition, 4th revised edition, von Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|zh|罗尔夫·克劳斯|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Incorrect ISBN|3-8067-8086-X|qq=}}. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977|qq=}}). '''Day of the Sand Holes''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Revise|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet-Festival|qq=}}) The article currently has no inline references.|qq=}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |EXPANDED-NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom of Egypt|qq=}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Shifting Sand / Collecting Sand''
''Festival of Pouring Sand'' |EXPANDED-NAME2 = '''Festival date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION =''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second lunar day of the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Pyramid of Senusret II at Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also '''Day of Collecting Sand''') is documented from the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom of Egypt|qq=}} under the reign of King ([[Pharaoh]]) [[:en:Nyuserre Ini|Nyuserre Ini]]. It is associated with the birth of [[:en:Horus|Horus]] and the goddess [[:en:Wosret|Wosret]], attested in [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom of Egypt]] at [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes, Egypt]], who was revered as a protector of the youthful Horus. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the commonly observed first day of the [[:en:New moon|New Moon]], but involved the [[:en:Ritual|ritual]] of ''filling the [[:en:Eye of Horus|Eye of Horus]]'' on the second lunar day. The {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet-Festival|qq=}} followed one day later on the third day, symbolizing the ''clothing of the [[:en:Moon|Moon]]''. === 12th Dynasty === In the [[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]], under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]], the festival was also named ''Day of Collecting Sand of Anubis on his mountain in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed''. The [[:en:Pyramid of Senusret II|Pyramid of Senusret II]] at [[:en:El Lahun|El Lahun]] was traditionally assigned to the ''Mountain of [[:en:Anubis|Anubis]]''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary temple of Senusret II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|センウセレト2世葬祭殿|zh|辛努塞尔特二世的太庙|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mortuary temple of Senusret II|qq=}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリン・パピルス10248|zh|柏林纸草文献10248|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Berlin 10248|qq=}}), the announcement is noted in the {{ill|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|古埃及阴历|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}} for the 14th regnal year of Senusret III on the [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Achet]] 18, second lunar day. Since the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|lt=Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|lt=Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|曲芸団の祭典暦|zh|杂技团节日历|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|qq=}} from the 35th regnal year of [[:en:Amenemhat III|Amenemhat III]] scheduled the Menchet festival for the second month of Achet without specifying the date, a lunar date announcement had not been available at the time of creating the festival calendar. ==Overview 2== {{Improve||This article|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Discussion: Menchet-Fest|de|Diskussion: Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭の議論|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Discussion: Menchet-Fest|qq=}}) The article does not yet contain any individual citation.|qq=}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |EXTENDED NAME = [[:en:Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom (Egypt)]] |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Sand Shifting / Sand Lifting''
''Festival of Sand Dispersion'' |EXTENDED NAME2 = '''Festival Date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION = ''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second day of the moon in the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Sesostris II Pyramid in Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also known as '''Day of Sand Lifting''') has been attested since the [[:en:Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom (Egypt)]] under the reign of King ([[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]]) [[:en:Nyuserre Ini|Niuserre]]. It is associated with the birth of [[:en:Horus|Horus]] and the goddess [[:en:Wosret|Wosret]], attested in the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom]] at [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes (Egypt)]], who was venerated as the protector of the youthful Horus. ==Background== ===Old Kingdom=== The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the otherwise customary first day of the [[:en:New moon|New Moon]], but instead included the [[:en:Ritual|Ritual]] of the ''Filling of the [[:en:Eye of Horus|Eye of Horus]]'' on the second day of the lunar month. The {{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet Festival|qq=}} followed one day later on the third day and symbolized the ''Dressing of the [[:en:Moon|Moon]]''. ===12th Dynasty=== In the [[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|12th Dynasty]], under [[:en:Senusret III|Sesostris III]], the designation ''Day of the Sand Lifting of [[:en:Anubis|Anubis]] on his mountain in the pyramid city of Sesostris II, the Blessed'' was also used. The [[:en:Pyramid of Senusret II|Sesostris II Pyramid]] of [[:en:El Lahun|Al-Lahun]] was traditionally assigned, by virtue of its location, to the ''Mountain of [[:en:Anubis|Anubis]]''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|セソストリスIIの葬祭寺|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|qq=}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリンパピルス10248|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Berlin 10248|qq=}}) it is recorded in the 14th regnal year of Sesostris III that the announcement in the {{ill|Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=}} for [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Achet]] on the second lunar day was made. Since in the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|lt=Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|lt=Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|アクロバット団の祭日暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|qq=}} from the 35th regnal year of [[:en:Amenemhat III|Amenemhet III]] the Menchet Festival for the second month Achet was scheduled without a specified date, an announcement of the lunar date was not yet available at the time of the creation of that festival calendar. ==Literature== * [[:en:Rainer Hannig|Rainer Hannig]]: ''Great Hand Dictionary of Egyptian – German. (2800 – 950 BC). The Language of the Pharaohs'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = {{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural History of the Ancient World|qq=}}. Volume 64). Marburg Edition, 4th revised edition, by Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Dates. It is associated with the birth of [[:en:Horus|Horus]] and the goddess [[:en:Wosret|Wosret]], attested in [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom of Egypt]] at [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes, Egypt]], who was revered as a protector of the youthful Horus. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the commonly observed first day of the [[:en:New moon|New Moon]], but involved the [[:en:Ritual|ritual]] of ''filling the [[:en:Eye of Horus|Eye of Horus]]'' on the second lunar day. The {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet-Festival|qq=}} followed one day later on the third day, symbolizing the ''clothing of the [[:en:Moon|Moon]]''. === 12th Dynasty === In the [[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]], under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]], the festival was also named ''Day of Collecting Sand of Anubis on his mountain in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed''. The [[:en:Pyramid of Senusret II|Pyramid of Senusret II]] at [[:en:El Lahun|El Lahun]] was traditionally assigned to the ''Mountain of [[:en:Anubis|Anubis]]''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary temple of Senusret II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|センウセレト2世葬祭殿|zh|辛努塞尔特二世的太庙|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mortuary temple of Senusret II|qq=}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリン・パピルス10248|zh|柏林纸草文献10248|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Berlin 10248|qq=}}), the announcement is noted in the {{ill|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|古埃及阴历|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|qq=}} for the 14th regnal year of Senusret III on the [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Achet]] 18, second lunar day. Since the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|lt=Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|lt=Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|曲芸団の祭典暦|zh|杂技团节日历|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|qq=}} from the 35th regnal year of [[:en:Amenemhat III|Amenemhat III]] scheduled the Menchet festival for the second month of Achet without specifying the date, a lunar date announcement had not been available at the time of creating the festival calendar. ==Overview 2== {{Improve||This article|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Discussion: Menchet-Fest|de|Diskussion: Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭の議論|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Discussion: Menchet-Fest|qq=}}) The article does not yet contain any individual citation.|qq=}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |EXTENDED NAME = [[:en:Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom (Egypt)]] |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Sand Shifting / Sand Lifting''
''Festival of Sand Dispersion'' |EXTENDED NAME2 = '''Festival Date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION = ''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second day of the moon in the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Sesostris II Pyramid in Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also known as '''Day of Sand Lifting''') has been attested since the [[:en:Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom (Egypt)]] under the reign of King ([[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]]) [[:en:Nyuserre Ini|Niuserre]]. It is associated with the birth of [[:en:Horus|Horus]] and the goddess [[:en:Wosret|Wosret]], attested in the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom]] at [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes (Egypt)]], who was venerated as the protector of the youthful Horus. ==Background== ===Old Kingdom=== The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the otherwise customary first day of the [[:en:New moon|New Moon]], but instead included the [[:en:Ritual|Ritual]] of the ''Filling of the [[:en:Eye of Horus|Eye of Horus]]'' on the second day of the lunar month. The {{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet Festival|qq=}} followed one day later on the third day and symbolized the ''Dressing of the [[:en:Moon|Moon]]''. ===12th Dynasty=== In the [[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|12th Dynasty]], under [[:en:Senusret III|Sesostris III]], the designation ''Day of the Sand Lifting of [[:en:Anubis|Anubis]] on his mountain in the pyramid city of Sesostris II, the Blessed'' was also used. The [[:en:Pyramid of Senusret II|Sesostris II Pyramid]] of [[:en:El Lahun|Al-Lahun]] was traditionally assigned, by virtue of its location, to the ''Mountain of [[:en:Anubis|Anubis]]''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|セソストリスIIの葬祭寺|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|qq=}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリンパピルス10248|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Berlin 10248|qq=}}) it is recorded in the 14th regnal year of Sesostris III that the announcement in the {{ill|Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=}} for [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Achet]] on the second lunar day was made. Since in the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|lt=Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|lt=Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|アクロバット団の祭日暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|qq=}} from the 35th regnal year of [[:en:Amenemhat III|Amenemhet III]] the Menchet Festival for the second month Achet was scheduled without a specified date, an announcement of the lunar date was not yet available at the time of the creation of that festival calendar. ==Literature== * [[:en:Rainer Hannig|Rainer Hannig]]: ''Great Hand Dictionary of Egyptian – German. (2800 – 950 BC). The Language of the Pharaohs'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = {{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural History of the Ancient World|qq=}}. Volume 64). Marburg Edition, 4th revised edition, by Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 649. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Sothis and Lunar Dates. '''Day of the Sand Holes''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== {{Revise|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet-Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) The article currently has no inline references.|qq=}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |EXPANDED-NAME = {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国}} |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Shifting Sand / Collecting Sand''
''Festival of Pouring Sand'' |EXPANDED-NAME2 = '''Festival date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION =''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second lunar day of the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Pyramid of Senusret II at Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also '''Day of Collecting Sand''') is documented from the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|de|Altes Reich (Ägypten)|ja|古王国|zh|古埃及王国|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom of Egypt|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国}} under the reign of King ([[Pharaoh]]) [[:en:Nyuserre Ini|Nyuserre Ini]]. It is associated with the birth of [[:en:Horus|Horus]] and the goddess [[:en:Wosret|Wosret]], attested in [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom of Egypt]] at [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes, Egypt]], who was revered as a protector of the youthful Horus. == Background == === Old Kingdom === The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the commonly observed first day of the [[:en:New moon|New Moon]], but involved the [[:en:Ritual|ritual]] of ''filling the [[:en:Eye of Horus|Eye of Horus]]'' on the second lunar day. The {{ill|Menchet-Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェト祭|zh|孟切特节|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet-Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} followed one day later on the third day, symbolizing the ''clothing of the [[:en:Moon|Moon]]''. === 12th Dynasty === In the [[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]], under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]], the festival was also named ''Day of Collecting Sand of Anubis on his mountain in the Pyramid City of Senusret II, the Blessed''. The [[:en:Pyramid of Senusret II|Pyramid of Senusret II]] at [[:en:El Lahun|El Lahun]] was traditionally assigned to the ''Mountain of [[:en:Anubis|Anubis]]''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary temple of Senusret II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|センウセレト2世葬祭殿|zh|辛努塞尔特二世的太庙|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mortuary temple of Senusret II|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリン・パピルス10248|zh|柏林纸草文献10248|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Berlin 10248|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), the announcement is noted in the {{ill|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|ja|エジプトの太陰暦|zh|古埃及阴历|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} for the 14th regnal year of Senusret III on the [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Achet]] 18, second lunar day. Since the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|lt=Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|lt=Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|曲芸団の祭典暦|zh|杂技团节日历|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from the 35th regnal year of [[:en:Amenemhat III|Amenemhat III]] scheduled the Menchet festival for the second month of Achet without specifying the date, a lunar date announcement had not been available at the time of creating the festival calendar. ==Overview 2== {{Improve||This article|reason=The dating must be corrected. (See discussion here and at {{ill|Discussion: Menchet-Fest|de|Diskussion: Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭の議論|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Discussion: Menchet-Fest|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) The article does not yet contain any individual citation.|qq=}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |EXTENDED NAME = [[:en:Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom (Egypt)]] |NAME = W3-Aa1:N35-Q3:D40-N37:D36-N33:Z2
Heb-chenep-scha
''Ḥb-ḫnp-šˁ''
''Festival of Sand Shifting / Sand Lifting''
''Festival of Sand Dispersion'' |EXTENDED NAME2 = '''Festival Date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t |NAME2-TRANSCRIPTION = ''3ḫt'' |NAME2-EXPLANATION = ''Second day of the moon in the second month of the Achet period'' |IMAGE1 = Pyramid at Lahun.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 300px |IMAGE1-DESCRIPTION = Sesostris II Pyramid in Al-Lahun }} The festival '''Chenep-scha''' (also known as '''Day of Sand Lifting''') has been attested since the [[:en:Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom (Egypt)]] under the reign of King ([[:en:Pharaoh|Pharaoh]]) [[:en:Nyuserre Ini|Niuserre]]. It is associated with the birth of [[:en:Horus|Horus]] and the goddess [[:en:Wosret|Wosret]], attested in the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom]] at [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes (Egypt)]], who was venerated as the protector of the youthful Horus. ==Background== ===Old Kingdom=== The Chenep-scha festival did not fall on the otherwise customary first day of the [[:en:New moon|New Moon]], but instead included the [[:en:Ritual|Ritual]] of the ''Filling of the [[:en:Eye of Horus|Eye of Horus]]'' on the second day of the lunar month. The {{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|ja|メンチェット祭|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} followed one day later on the third day and symbolized the ''Dressing of the [[:en:Moon|Moon]]''. ===12th Dynasty=== In the [[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|12th Dynasty]], under [[:en:Senusret III|Sesostris III]], the designation ''Day of the Sand Lifting of [[:en:Anubis|Anubis]] on his mountain in the pyramid city of Sesostris II, the Blessed'' was also used. The [[:en:Pyramid of Senusret II|Sesostris II Pyramid]] of [[:en:El Lahun|Al-Lahun]] was traditionally assigned, by virtue of its location, to the ''Mountain of [[:en:Anubis|Anubis]]''. From the diary of the {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|de|Totentempel des Sesostris II.|ja|セソストリスIIの葬祭寺|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mortuary Temple of Sesostris II|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10248|de|Papyrus Berlin 10248|ja|ベルリンパピルス10248|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Berlin 10248|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) it is recorded in the 14th regnal year of Sesostris III that the announcement in the {{ill|Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja|古代エジプトの月暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunar Calendar (Ancient Egypt)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} for [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Achet]] on the second lunar day was made. Since in the {{ill|Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|lt=Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|lt=Festival calendar of the acrobat troupe|de|Festkalender der Akrobatentruppe|ja|アクロバット団の祭日暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival Calendar of the Acrobat Troupe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from the 35th regnal year of [[:en:Amenemhat III|Amenemhet III]] the Menchet Festival for the second month Achet was scheduled without a specified date, an announcement of the lunar date was not yet available at the time of the creation of that festival calendar. ==Literature== * [[:en:Rainer Hannig|Rainer Hannig]]: ''Great Hand Dictionary of Egyptian – German. (2800 – 950 BC). The Language of the Pharaohs'' (= ''Hannig-Lexica.'' Volume 1 = {{ill|Cultural History of the Ancient World|de|Kulturgeschichte der Antiken Welt|ja|古代世界の文化史|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural History of the Ancient World|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Volume 64).
